authors_text: Carlos A. Diaz-Ruiz,Youya Xia,Yurong You,Jose Nino,Junan Chen,Josephine Monica,Xiangyu Chen,Katie Luo,Yan Wang,Marc Emond,Wei-Lun Chao,Bharath Hariharan,Kilian Q. Weinberger,Mark Campbell
title: Ithaca365: Dataset and Driving Perception under Repeated and Challenging Weather Conditions
title_zh: Ithaca365：反复且具有挑战性的天气条件下的数据集和驾驶感知
abstract: Advances in perception for self-driving cars have accelerated in recent years due to the availability of large-scale datasets, typically collected at specific locations and under nice weather conditions. Yet, to achieve the high safety requirement, these perceptual systems must operate robustly under a wide variety of weather conditions including snow and rain. In this paper, we present a new dataset to enable robust autonomous driving via a novel data collection process - data is repeatedly recorded along a 15 km route under diverse scene (urban, highway, rural, campus), weather (snow, rain, sun), time (day/night), and traffic conditions (pedestrians, cyclists and cars). The dataset includes images and point clouds from cameras and LiDAR sensors, along with high-precision GPS/INS to establish correspondence across routes. The dataset includes road and object annotations using amodal masks to capture partial occlusions and 3D bounding boxes. We demonstrate the uniqueness of this dataset by analyzing the performance of baselines in amodal segmentation of road and objects, depth estimation, and 3D object detection. The repeated routes opens new research directions in object discovery, continual learning, and anomaly detection. Link to Ithaca365: https://ithaca365.mae.cornell.edu/
summary: Advances in perception for self-driving cars have accelerated in recent years due to the availability of large-scale datasets, typically collected at specific locations and under nice weather conditions. Yet, to achieve the high safety requirement, these perceptual systems must operate robustly under a wide variety of weather conditions including snow and rain. In this paper, we present a new dataset to enable robust autonomous driving via a novel data collection process - data is repeatedly recorded along a 15 km route under diverse scene (urban, highway, rural, campus), weather (snow, rain, sun), time (day/night), and traffic conditions (pedestrians, cyclists and cars). The dataset includes images and point clouds from cameras and LiDAR sensors, along with high-precision GPS/INS to establish correspondence across routes. The dataset includes road and object annotations using amodal masks to capture partial occlusions and 3D bounding boxes. We demonstrate the uniqueness of this dataset by analyzing the performance of baselines in amodal segmentation of road and objects, depth estimation, and 3D object detection. The repeated routes opens new research directions in object discovery, continual learning, and anomaly detection. Link to Ithaca365: https://ithaca365.mae.cornell.edu/
abstract_zh: 近年来，由于大规模数据集的可用性（通常在特定地点和良好的天气条件下收集），自动驾驶汽车的感知进步加速。然而，为了达到高安全要求，这些感知系统必须在包括雪和雨在内的各种天气条件下稳健运行。在本文中，我们提出了一个新的数据集，通过新颖的数据收集过程实现强大的自动驾驶——在不同场景（城市、高速公路、农村、校园）、天气（雪、雨、阳光）、时间（白天/夜间）和交通条件（行人、骑自行车者和汽车）下，沿着 15 公里的路线重复记录数据。该数据集包括来自摄像机和 LiDAR 传感器的图像和点云，以及用于建立跨路线对应的高精度 GPS/INS。该数据集包括使用模态掩模捕获部分遮挡和 3D 边界框的道路和对象注释。我们通过分析道路和物体的非模态分割、深度估计和 3D 物体检测中的基线性能，证明了该数据集的独特性。重复的路线在对象发现、持续学习和异常检测方面开辟了新的研究方向。链接到 Ithaca365：https://ithaca365.mae.cornell.edu/

AI.tldr: 本文提出Ithaca365数据集，通过重复记录15公里路线下的多样化场景、天气和交通条件，推动自动驾驶在恶劣天气下的感知研究。
AI.motivation: 现有自动驾驶数据集多局限于特定地点和良好天气，难以满足复杂天气条件下的安全需求，需构建更具挑战性的数据集以提升系统鲁棒性。
AI.method: 沿15公里路线重复采集数据，涵盖城市、高速等场景及雪、雨等天气，结合摄像头、激光雷达和高精度GPS/INS，并采用模态掩码和3D边界框进行标注。
AI.result: 通过基线模型在道路分割、深度估计和3D目标检测中的表现验证数据集独特性，重复路线设计为持续学习和异常检测提供新研究方向。
AI.conclusion: Ithaca365数据集为复杂天气下的自动驾驶感知研究提供关键资源，推动面向真实场景的算法优化与创新方法探索。

authors_text: Jicheng Li,Anjana Bhat,Roghayeh Barmaki
title: Pose Uncertainty Aware Movement Synchrony Estimation via Spatial-Temporal Graph Transformer
title_zh: 通过时空图转换器进行姿势不确定性运动同步估计
comment: Accepted by 24th ACM International Conference on Multimodal Interaction (ICMI'22). 17 pages, 2 figures
comments: Accepted by 24th ACM International Conference on Multimodal Interaction (ICMI'22). 17 pages, 2 figures
abstract: Movement synchrony reflects the coordination of body movements between interacting dyads. The estimation of movement synchrony has been automated by powerful deep learning models such as transformer networks. However, instead of designing a specialized network for movement synchrony estimation, previous transformer-based works broadly adopted architectures from other tasks such as human activity recognition. Therefore, this paper proposed a skeleton-based graph transformer for movement synchrony estimation. The proposed model applied ST-GCN, a spatial-temporal graph convolutional neural network for skeleton feature extraction, followed by a spatial transformer for spatial feature generation. The spatial transformer is guided by a uniquely designed joint position embedding shared between the same joints of interacting individuals. Besides, we incorporated a temporal similarity matrix in temporal attention computation considering the periodic intrinsic of body movements. In addition, the confidence score associated with each joint reflects the uncertainty of a pose, while previous works on movement synchrony estimation have not sufficiently emphasized this point. Since transformer networks demand a significant amount of data to train, we constructed a dataset for movement synchrony estimation using Human3.6M, a benchmark dataset for human activity recognition, and pretrained our model on it using contrastive learning. We further applied knowledge distillation to alleviate information loss introduced by pose detector failure in a privacy-preserving way. We compared our method with representative approaches on PT13, a dataset collected from autism therapy interventions. Our method achieved an overall accuracy of 88.98% and surpassed its counterparts by a wide margin while maintaining data privacy.

The estimation of movement synchrony has been automated by powerful deep learning models such as transformer networks. However, instead of designing a specialized network for movement synchrony estimation, previous transformer-based works broadly adopted architectures from other tasks such as human activity recognition. Therefore, this paper proposed a skeleton-based graph transformer for movement synchrony estimation. The proposed model applied ST-GCN, a spatial-temporal graph convolutional neural network for skeleton feature extraction, followed by a spatial transformer for spatial feature generation. The spatial transformer is guided by a uniquely designed joint position embedding shared between the same joints of interacting individuals. Besides, we incorporated a temporal similarity matrix in temporal attention computation considering the periodic intrinsic of body movements. In addition, the confidence score associated with each joint reflects the uncertainty of a pose, while previous works on movement synchrony estimation have not sufficiently emphasized this point. Since transformer networks demand a significant amount of data to train, we constructed a dataset for movement synchrony estimation using Human3.6M, a benchmark dataset for human activity recognition, and pretrained our model on it using contrastive learning. We further applied knowledge distillation to alleviate information loss introduced by pose detector failure in a privacy-preserving way. We compared our method with representative approaches on PT13, a dataset collected from autism therapy interventions. Our method achieved an overall accuracy of 88.98% and surpassed its counterparts by a wide margin while maintaining data privacy.
abstract_zh: 运动同步性反映了相互作用的二元体之间身体运动的协调性。运动同步的估计已通过强大的深度学习模型（例如变压器网络）实现自动化。然而，之前基于 Transformer 的工作并没有设计专门的网络来进行运动同步估计，而是广泛采用了其他任务（例如人类活动识别）的架构。因此，本文提出了一种基于骨架的图变换器用于运动同步估计。所提出的模型应用 ST-GCN（一种时空图卷积神经网络）进行骨架特征提取，然后使用空间变换器进行空间特征生成。空间变换器由交互个体的相同关节之间共享的独特设计的关节位置嵌入引导。此外，考虑到身体运动的周期性，我们在时间注意力计算中加入了时间相似性矩阵。此外，与每个关节相关的置信度分数反映了姿势的不确定性，而先前关于运动同步估计的工作并没有充分强调这一点。由于 Transformer 网络需要大量数据进行训练，因此我们使用人类活动识别的基准数据集 Human3.6M 构建了一个用于运动同步估计的数据集，并使用对比学习在其上预训练了我们的模型。我们进一步应用知识蒸馏来以保护隐私的方式减轻姿势检测器故障带来的信息丢失。我们将我们的方法与 PT13 上的代表性方法进行了比较，PT13 是从自闭症治疗干预措施中收集的数据集。我们的方法的总体准确率达到 88.98%，在保持数据隐私的同时大幅超越了同类方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于骨骼的图变压器模型，用于估计互动双人之间的运动同步性，并考虑姿态不确定性。
AI.motivation: 现有基于变压器的运动同步性估计方法未专门设计网络结构，且忽视了姿态置信度对同步性估计的影响。
AI.method: 结合ST-GCN提取骨骼特征，设计共享关节位置嵌入的空间变压器，引入时间相似矩阵计算时序注意力，并通过对比学习和知识蒸馏处理隐私数据。
AI.result: 在PT13数据集上达到88.98%的总体准确率，显著优于现有方法且保持数据隐私。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过融合空间-时序图变压器与姿态不确定性建模，有效提升了运动同步性估计性能。

authors_text: Yandong Ji,Zhongyu Li,Yinan Sun,Xue Bin Peng,Sergey Levine,Glen Berseth,Koushil Sreenath
title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Precise Soccer Shooting Skills using a Quadrupedal Robot
title_zh: 使用四足机器人进行精确足球射门技能的分层强化学习
comment: Accepted to 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022)
comments: Accepted to 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS 2022)
abstract: We address the problem of enabling quadrupedal robots to perform precise shooting skills in the real world using reinforcement learning. Developing algorithms to enable a legged robot to shoot a soccer ball to a given target is a challenging problem that combines robot motion control and planning into one task. To solve this problem, we need to consider the dynamics limitation and motion stability during the control of a dynamic legged robot. Moreover, we need to consider motion planning to shoot the hard-to-model deformable ball rolling on the ground with uncertain friction to a desired location. In this paper, we propose a hierarchical framework that leverages deep reinforcement learning to train (a) a robust motion control policy that can track arbitrary motions and (b) a planning policy to decide the desired kicking motion to shoot a soccer ball to a target. We deploy the proposed framework on an A1 quadrupedal robot and enable it to accurately shoot the ball to random targets in the real world.
summary: We address the problem of enabling quadrupedal robots to perform precise shooting skills in the real world using reinforcement learning. Developing algorithms to enable a legged robot to shoot a soccer ball to a given target is a challenging problem that combines robot motion control and planning into one task. To solve this problem, we need to consider the dynamics limitation and motion stability during the control of a dynamic legged robot. Moreover, we need to consider motion planning to shoot the hard-to-model deformable ball rolling on the ground with uncertain friction to a desired location. In this paper, we propose a hierarchical framework that leverages deep reinforcement learning to train (a) a robust motion control policy that can track arbitrary motions and (b) a planning policy to decide the desired kicking motion to shoot a soccer ball to a target. We deploy the proposed framework on an A1 quadrupedal robot and enable it to accurately shoot the ball to random targets in the real world.
abstract_zh: 我们使用强化学习解决了使四足机器人在现实世界中执行精确射击技能的问题。开发算法使腿式机器人能够将足球射向给定目标是一个具有挑战性的问题，它将机器人运动控制和规划结合到一项任务中。为了解决这个问题，我们需要考虑动态腿机器人控制过程中的动力学限制和运动稳定性。此外，我们需要考虑运动规划，将难以建模的可变形球在地面上以不确定的摩擦力滚动到所需的位置。在本文中，我们提出了一个分层框架，利用深度强化学习来训练（a）可以跟踪任意运动的稳健运动控制策略，以及（b）决定将足球射向目标所需的踢球运动的规划策略。我们将所提出的框架部署在 A1 四足机器人上，使其能够准确地将球射向现实世界中的随机目标。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层强化学习框架，使四足机器人能够实现精准足球射门技能。
AI.motivation: 四足机器人射门需同时解决动态运动控制、规划及不确定地面摩擦下的变形球建模难题，传统方法难以兼顾稳定性与精度。
AI.method: 设计分层架构，上层规划策略决定目标射门动作，下层控制策略通过深度强化学习实现动态运动跟踪与稳定控制。
AI.result: 成功在A1四足机器人上实现对随机目标的精准射门，验证了框架在实际场景中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 分层强化学习为复杂动态场景下的机器人运动控制与规划提供了新方法，推动了腿式机器人精准操作能力的发展。

search_text: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Precise Soccer Shooting Skills using a Quadrupedal Robot 使用四足机器人进行精确足球射门技能的分层强化学习 We address the problem of enabling quadrupedal robots to perform precise shooting skills in the real world using reinforcement learning. Developing algorithms to enable a legged robot to shoot a soccer ball to a given target is a challenging problem that combines robot motion control and planning into one task. To solve this problem, we need to consider the dynamics limitation and motion stability during the control of a dynamic legged robot. Moreover, we need to consider motion planning to shoot the hard-to-model deformable ball rolling on the ground with uncertain friction to a desired location. In this paper, we propose a hierarchical framework that leverages deep reinforcement learning to train (a) a robust motion control policy that can track arbitrary motions and (b) a planning policy to decide the desired kicking motion to shoot a soccer ball to a target. We deploy the proposed framework on an A1 quadrupedal robot and enable it to accurately shoot the ball to random targets in the real world. 我们使用强化学习解决了使四足机器人在现实世界中执行精确射击技能的问题。开发算法使腿式机器人能够将足球射向给定目标是一个具有挑战性的问题，它将机器人运动控制和规划结合到一项任务中。为了解决这个问题，我们需要考虑动态腿机器人控制过程中的动力学限制和运动稳定性。此外，我们需要考虑运动规划，将难以建模的可变形球在地面上以不确定的摩擦力滚动到所需的位置。在本文中，我们提出了一个分层框架，利用深度强化学习来训练（a）可以跟踪任意运动的稳健运动控制策略，以及（b）决定将足球射向目标所需的踢球运动的规划策略。我们将所提出的框架部署在 A1 四足机器人上，使其能够准确地将球射向现实世界中的随机目标。 本文提出一种分层强化学习框架，使四足机器人能够实现精准足球射门技能。 Yandong Ji,Zhongyu Li,Yinan Sun,Xue Bin Peng,Sergey Levine,Glen Berseth,Koushil Sreenath cs.RO,cs.AI,eess.SY

authors_text: Ye Yu,Jialin Yuan,Gaurav Mittal,Li Fuxin,Mei Chen
title: BATMAN: Bilateral Attention Transformer in Motion-Appearance Neighboring Space for Video Object Segmentation
title_zh: BATMAN：用于视频对象分割的运动外观邻近空间中的双边注意力变换器
abstract: Video Object Segmentation (VOS) is fundamental to video understanding. Transformer-based methods show significant performance improvement on semi-supervised VOS. However, existing work faces challenges segmenting visually similar objects in close proximity of each other. In this paper, we propose a novel Bilateral Attention Transformer in Motion-Appearance Neighboring space (BATMAN) for semi-supervised VOS. It captures object motion in the video via a novel optical flow calibration module that fuses the segmentation mask with optical flow estimation to improve within-object optical flow smoothness and reduce noise at object boundaries. This calibrated optical flow is then employed in our novel bilateral attention, which computes the correspondence between the query and reference frames in the neighboring bilateral space considering both motion and appearance. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of BATMAN architecture by outperforming all existing state-of-the-art on all four popular VOS benchmarks: Youtube-VOS 2019 (85.0%), Youtube-VOS 2018 (85.3%), DAVIS 2017Val/Testdev (86.2%/82.2%), and DAVIS 2016 (92.5%).
summary: Video Object Segmentation (VOS) is fundamental to video understanding. Transformer-based methods show significant performance improvement on semi-supervised VOS. However, existing work faces challenges segmenting visually similar objects in close proximity of each other. In this paper, we propose a novel Bilateral Attention Transformer in Motion-Appearance Neighboring space (BATMAN) for semi-supervised VOS. It captures object motion in the video via a novel optical flow calibration module that fuses the segmentation mask with optical flow estimation to improve within-object optical flow smoothness and reduce noise at object boundaries. This calibrated optical flow is then employed in our novel bilateral attention, which computes the correspondence between the query and reference frames in the neighboring bilateral space considering both motion and appearance. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of BATMAN architecture by outperforming all existing state-of-the-art on all four popular VOS benchmarks: Youtube-VOS 2019 (85.0%), Youtube-VOS 2018 (85.3%), DAVIS 2017Val/Testdev (86.2%/82.2%), and DAVIS 2016 (92.5%).
abstract_zh: 视频对象分割 (VOS) 是视频理解的基础。基于 Transformer 的方法在半监督 VOS 上显示出显着的性能改进。然而，现有的工作面临着分割视觉上相似的彼此接近的物体的挑战。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于半监督 VOS 的运动外观邻近空间 (BATMAN) 中的新型双边注意力变换器。它通过新颖的光流校准模块捕获视频中的对象运动，该模块将分割掩模与光流估计融合，以提高对象内光流平滑度并减少对象边界处的噪声。然后，这种校准的光流被用于我们新颖的双边注意力中，它考虑运动和外观，计算相邻双边空间中查询帧和参考帧之间的对应关系。大量实验验证了 BATMAN 架构的有效性，它在所有四个流行的 VOS 基准测试中均优于所有现有的最新技术：Youtube-VOS 2019 (85.0%)、Youtube-VOS 2018 (85.3%)、DAVIS 2017Val/Testdev (86.2%/82.2%) 和 DAVIS 2016 (92.5%)。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BATMAN模型，通过改进半监督视频目标分割中相邻相似物体的分割效果，提升视频理解性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在分割视觉相似且邻近的物体时存在挑战，需改进分割精度与边界噪声处理。
AI.method: 设计光学流校准模块融合分割掩码与光流估计，结合双侧注意力机制在运动-外观邻域空间计算帧间对应关系。
AI.result: 在四个主流基准数据集上均超越现有最优方法，DAVIS 2016达92.5% J&F指标。
AI.conclusion: BATMAN通过双侧注意力与光流校准有效解决了邻近相似物体分割难题，验证了架构创新的有效性。

authors_text: Matthew McDermott,Jason Rife
title: Mitigating Shadows in Lidar Scan Matching using Spherical Voxels
title_zh: 使用球形体素减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中的阴影
abstract: In this paper we propose an approach to mitigate shadowing errors in Lidar scan matching, by introducing a preprocessing step based on spherical gridding. Because the grid aligns with the Lidar beam, it is relatively easy to eliminate shadow edges which cause systematic errors in Lidar scan matching. As we show through simulation, our proposed algorithm provides better results than ground-plane removal, the most common existing strategy for shadow mitigation. Unlike ground plane removal, our method applies to arbitrary terrains (e.g. shadows on urban walls, shadows in hilly terrain) while retaining key Lidar points on the ground that are critical for estimating changes in height, pitch, and roll. Our preprocessing algorithm can be used with a range of scan-matching methods; however, for voxel-based scan matching methods, it provides additional benefits by reducing computation costs and more evenly distributing Lidar points among voxels.
summary: In this paper we propose an approach to mitigate shadowing errors in Lidar scan matching, by introducing a preprocessing step based on spherical gridding. Because the grid aligns with the Lidar beam, it is relatively easy to eliminate shadow edges which cause systematic errors in Lidar scan matching. As we show through simulation, our proposed algorithm provides better results than ground-plane removal, the most common existing strategy for shadow mitigation. Unlike ground plane removal, our method applies to arbitrary terrains (e.g. shadows on urban walls, shadows in hilly terrain) while retaining key Lidar points on the ground that are critical for estimating changes in height, pitch, and roll. Our preprocessing algorithm can be used with a range of scan-matching methods; however, for voxel-based scan matching methods, it provides additional benefits by reducing computation costs and more evenly distributing Lidar points among voxels.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种通过引入基于球形网格的预处理步骤来减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中的阴影误差的方法。由于网格与激光雷达光束对齐，因此相对容易消除在激光雷达扫描匹配中引起系统误差的阴影边缘。正如我们通过模拟所示，我们提出的算法提供了比地平面去除更好的结果，地平面去除是最常见的现有阴影缓解策略。与地平面去除不同，我们的方法适用于任意地形（例如城市墙壁上的阴影、丘陵地形中的阴影），同时保留地面上的关键激光雷达点，这些点对于估计高度、俯仰和滚动的变化至关重要。我们的预处理算法可与一系列扫描匹配方法一起使用；然而，对于基于体素的扫描匹配方法，它通过降低计算成本和在体素之间更均匀地分布激光雷达点来提供额外的好处。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用球形体素预处理来减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中阴影误差的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有阴影消除方法（如地面平面移除）在复杂地形中效果有限，且可能丢失关键地面点信息。
AI.method: 通过与激光雷达光束对齐的球形网格预处理，消除导致系统误差的阴影边缘。
AI.result: 仿真表明该算法优于传统方法，适用于任意地形且保留关键地面点，同时降低体素匹配方法的计算成本。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在阴影消除效果和地形适应性上优于现有技术，可广泛应用于不同扫描匹配算法。

result: 仿真表明该算法优于传统方法，适用于任意地形且保留关键地面点，同时降低体素匹配方法的计算成本。
conclusion: 该方法在阴影消除效果和地形适应性上优于现有技术，可广泛应用于不同扫描匹配算法。
search_text: Mitigating Shadows in Lidar Scan Matching using Spherical Voxels 使用球形体素减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中的阴影 In this paper we propose an approach to mitigate shadowing errors in Lidar scan matching, by introducing a preprocessing step based on spherical gridding. Because the grid aligns with the Lidar beam, it is relatively easy to eliminate shadow edges which cause systematic errors in Lidar scan matching. As we show through simulation, our proposed algorithm provides better results than ground-plane removal, the most common existing strategy for shadow mitigation. Unlike ground plane removal, our method applies to arbitrary terrains (e.g. shadows on urban walls, shadows in hilly terrain) while retaining key Lidar points on the ground that are critical for estimating changes in height, pitch, and roll. Our preprocessing algorithm can be used with a range of scan-matching methods; however, for voxel-based scan matching methods, it provides additional benefits by reducing computation costs and more evenly distributing Lidar points among voxels. 在本文中，我们提出了一种通过引入基于球形网格的预处理步骤来减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中的阴影误差的方法。由于网格与激光雷达光束对齐，因此相对容易消除在激光雷达扫描匹配中引起系统误差的阴影边缘。正如我们通过模拟所示，我们提出的算法提供了比地平面去除更好的结果，地平面去除是最常见的现有阴影缓解策略。与地平面去除不同，我们的方法适用于任意地形（例如城市墙壁上的阴影、丘陵地形中的阴影），同时保留地面上的关键激光雷达点，这些点对于估计高度、俯仰和滚动的变化至关重要。我们的预处理算法可与一系列扫描匹配方法一起使用；然而，对于基于体素的扫描匹配方法，它通过降低计算成本和在体素之间更均匀地分布激光雷达点来提供额外的好处。 本文提出一种利用球形体素预处理来减轻激光雷达扫描匹配中阴影误差的方法。 Matthew McDermott,Jason Rife cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Shuang Chen,Amir Atapour-Abarghouei,Jane Kerby,Edmond S. L. Ho,David C. G. Sainsbury,Sophie Butterworth,Hubert P. H. Shum
title: A Feasibility Study on Image Inpainting for Non-cleft Lip Generation from Patients with Cleft Lip
title_zh: 唇裂患者图像修复修复唇裂的可行性研究
abstract: A Cleft lip is a congenital abnormality requiring surgical repair by a specialist. The surgeon must have extensive experience and theoretical knowledge to perform surgery, and Artificial Intelligence (AI) method has been proposed to guide surgeons in improving surgical outcomes. If AI can be used to predict what a repaired cleft lip would look like, surgeons could use it as an adjunct to adjust their surgical technique and improve results. To explore the feasibility of this idea while protecting patient privacy, we propose a deep learning-based image inpainting method that is capable of covering a cleft lip and generating a lip and nose without a cleft. Our experiments are conducted on two real-world cleft lip datasets and are assessed by expert cleft lip surgeons to demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed method.
summary: A Cleft lip is a congenital abnormality requiring surgical repair by a specialist. The surgeon must have extensive experience and theoretical knowledge to perform surgery, and Artificial Intelligence (AI) method has been proposed to guide surgeons in improving surgical outcomes. If AI can be used to predict what a repaired cleft lip would look like, surgeons could use it as an adjunct to adjust their surgical technique and improve results. To explore the feasibility of this idea while protecting patient privacy, we propose a deep learning-based image inpainting method that is capable of covering a cleft lip and generating a lip and nose without a cleft. Our experiments are conducted on two real-world cleft lip datasets and are assessed by expert cleft lip surgeons to demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: 唇裂是一种先天性异常，需要专家进行手术修复。外科医生必须拥有丰富的经验和理论知识才能进行手术，人工智能（AI）方法已被提出来指导外科医生改善手术结果。如果人工智能可以用来预测修复后的唇裂会是什么样子，外科医生就可以用它作为辅助手段来调整手术技术并改善结果。为了探索这一想法的可行性，同时保护患者隐私，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的图像修复方法，能够覆盖唇裂并生成无裂唇和鼻子。我们的实验是在两个真实世界的唇裂数据集上进行的，并由唇裂外科医生专家进行评估，以证明所提出方法的可行性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的图像修复方法，用于生成无唇裂的唇部和鼻部图像，以辅助唇裂修复手术。
AI.motivation: 唇裂手术需依赖医生经验，而AI辅助预测修复效果可提升手术质量并保护患者隐私。
AI.method: 采用深度学习图像修复技术，覆盖唇裂区域生成正常唇鼻图像，并在真实数据集上进行验证。
AI.result: 实验结果通过专家评估验证了该方法在生成无唇裂图像方面的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为唇裂手术提供了可行的AI辅助方案，在保护隐私的同时优化手术效果。

authors_text: Stefan Lattner
title: SampleMatch: Drum Sample Retrieval by Musical Context
title_zh: SampleMatch：通过音乐背景检索鼓样本
comment: 8 pages, 3 figures, 1 table; Accepted at the ISMIR conference, Bengaluru, India, 2022
comments: 8 pages, 3 figures, 1 table; Accepted at the ISMIR conference, Bengaluru, India, 2022
abstract: Modern digital music production typically involves combining numerous acoustic elements to compile a piece of music. Important types of such elements are drum samples, which determine the characteristics of the percussive components of the piece. Artists must use their aesthetic judgement to assess whether a given drum sample fits the current musical context. However, selecting drum samples from a potentially large library is tedious and may interrupt the creative flow. In this work, we explore the automatic drum sample retrieval based on aesthetic principles learned from data. As a result, artists can rank the samples in their library by fit to some musical context at different stages of the production process (i.e., by fit to incomplete song mixtures). To this end, we use contrastive learning to maximize the score of drum samples originating from the same song as the mixture. We conduct a listening test to determine whether the human ratings match the automatic scoring function. We also perform objective quantitative analyses to evaluate the efficacy of our approach.
summary: Modern digital music production typically involves combining numerous acoustic elements to compile a piece of music. Important types of such elements are drum samples, which determine the characteristics of the percussive components of the piece. Artists must use their aesthetic judgement to assess whether a given drum sample fits the current musical context. However, selecting drum samples from a potentially large library is tedious and may interrupt the creative flow. In this work, we explore the automatic drum sample retrieval based on aesthetic principles learned from data. As a result, artists can rank the samples in their library by fit to some musical context at different stages of the production process (i.e., by fit to incomplete song mixtures). To this end, we use contrastive learning to maximize the score of drum samples originating from the same song as the mixture. We conduct a listening test to determine whether the human ratings match the automatic scoring function. We also perform objective quantitative analyses to evaluate the efficacy of our approach.
abstract_zh: 现代数字音乐制作通常涉及结合众多声学元素来编写一首音乐。这些元素的重要类型是鼓样本，它决定了乐曲打击乐成分的特征。艺术家必须利用他们的审美判断来评估给定的鼓样本是否适合当前的音乐背景。然而，从可能很大的库中选择鼓样本是乏味的，并且可能会中断创意流程。在这项工作中，我们探索基于从数据中学习的美学原理的自动鼓样本检索。因此，艺术家可以通过适合制作过程不同阶段的某些音乐背景（即，适合不完整的歌曲混合物）来对库中的样本进行排名。为此，我们使用对比学习来最大化源自同一首歌曲作为混合物的鼓样本的分数。我们进行听力测试，以确定人工评分是否与自动评分功能匹配。我们还进行客观的定量分析来评估我们方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SampleMatch方法，通过音乐上下文自动检索匹配的鼓点样本，以提升数字音乐制作效率。
AI.motivation: 艺术家在创作过程中需从大量鼓点样本库中手动挑选合适样本，此过程耗时且可能打断创作灵感。
AI.method: 采用对比学习方法，通过最大化同一歌曲来源的鼓点样本评分，结合听觉测试与定量分析验证效果。
AI.result: 听觉测试显示自动评分与人类评价一致，客观分析证实该方法能有效提升样本匹配准确性。
AI.conclusion: SampleMatch通过数据驱动的美学原则，为艺术家提供符合音乐语境的鼓点样本检索方案，优化创作流程。

authors_text: Fangjun Li,David C. Hogg,Anthony G. Cohn
title: Exploring the GLIDE model for Human Action-effect Prediction
title_zh: 探索用于人类行为效果预测的 GLIDE 模型
abstract: We address the following action-effect prediction task. Given an image depicting an initial state of the world and an action expressed in text, predict an image depicting the state of the world following the action. The prediction should have the same scene context as the input image. We explore the use of the recently proposed GLIDE model for performing this task. GLIDE is a generative neural network that can synthesize (inpaint) masked areas of an image, conditioned on a short piece of text. Our idea is to mask-out a region of the input image where the effect of the action is expected to occur. GLIDE is then used to inpaint the masked region conditioned on the required action. In this way, the resulting image has the same background context as the input image, updated to show the effect of the action. We give qualitative results from experiments using the EPIC dataset of ego-centric videos labelled with actions.
summary: We address the following action-effect prediction task. Given an image depicting an initial state of the world and an action expressed in text, predict an image depicting the state of the world following the action. The prediction should have the same scene context as the input image. We explore the use of the recently proposed GLIDE model for performing this task. GLIDE is a generative neural network that can synthesize (inpaint) masked areas of an image, conditioned on a short piece of text. Our idea is to mask-out a region of the input image where the effect of the action is expected to occur. GLIDE is then used to inpaint the masked region conditioned on the required action. In this way, the resulting image has the same background context as the input image, updated to show the effect of the action. We give qualitative results from experiments using the EPIC dataset of ego-centric videos labelled with actions.
abstract_zh: 我们解决以下行动效果预测任务。给定一个描述世界初始状态的图像和一个以文本表达的动作，预测一个描述该动作后世界状态的图像。预测应具有与输入图像相同的场景上下文。我们探索使用最近提出的 GLIDE 模型来执行此任务。 GLIDE 是一种生成神经网络，可以根据一小段文本合成（修复）图像的遮罩区域。我们的想法是屏蔽掉输入图像中预期发生动作效果的区域。然后使用 GLIDE 根据所需的操作修复遮罩区域。通过这种方式，生成的图像具有与输入图像相同的背景上下文，并更新以显示操作的效果。我们使用带有动作标签的以自我为中心的视频 EPIC 数据集进行实验，给出了定性结果。

AI.tldr: 本文探索使用GLIDE模型进行人类动作效果预测，通过输入初始状态图像和文本动作，生成反映动作效果的图像。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在预测动作效果时难以保持输入图像的场景上下文一致性，需开发能保留背景并准确生成动作结果的模型。
AI.method: 通过遮罩图像中动作效果预期区域，利用GLIDE模型根据文本动作描述进行图像修复（inpainting），生成包含动作效果且保留原始场景背景的图像。
AI.result: 在EPIC数据集上的实验展示了生成图像在保持场景上下文的同时，能够合理呈现动作效果的定性结果。
AI.conclusion: GLIDE模型通过图像修复技术有效实现了动作效果预测任务，为保持场景一致性提供了可行方案，但需进一步量化评估其性能。

ai_content.tldr: 本文探索使用GLIDE模型进行人类动作效果预测，通过输入初始状态图像和文本动作，生成反映动作效果的图像。

ai_content.result: 在EPIC数据集上的实验展示了生成图像在保持场景上下文的同时，能够合理呈现动作效果的定性结果。
ai_content.conclusion: GLIDE模型通过图像修复技术有效实现了动作效果预测任务，为保持场景一致性提供了可行方案，但需进一步量化评估其性能。

search_text: Exploring the GLIDE model for Human Action-effect Prediction 探索用于人类行为效果预测的 GLIDE 模型 We address the following action-effect prediction task. Given an image depicting an initial state of the world and an action expressed in text, predict an image depicting the state of the world following the action. The prediction should have the same scene context as the input image. We explore the use of the recently proposed GLIDE model for performing this task. GLIDE is a generative neural network that can synthesize (inpaint) masked areas of an image, conditioned on a short piece of text. Our idea is to mask-out a region of the input image where the effect of the action is expected to occur. GLIDE is then used to inpaint the masked region conditioned on the required action. In this way, the resulting image has the same background context as the input image, updated to show the effect of the action. We give qualitative results from experiments using the EPIC dataset of ego-centric videos labelled with actions. 我们解决以下行动效果预测任务。给定一个描述世界初始状态的图像和一个以文本表达的动作，预测一个描述该动作后世界状态的图像。预测应具有与输入图像相同的场景上下文。我们探索使用最近提出的 GLIDE 模型来执行此任务。 GLIDE 是一种生成神经网络，可以根据一小段文本合成（修复）图像的遮罩区域。我们的想法是屏蔽掉输入图像中预期发生动作效果的区域。然后使用 GLIDE 根据所需的操作修复遮罩区域。通过这种方式，生成的图像具有与输入图像相同的背景上下文，并更新以显示操作的效果。我们使用带有动作标签的以自我为中心的视频 EPIC 数据集进行实验，给出了定性结果。 本文探索使用GLIDE模型进行人类动作效果预测，通过输入初始状态图像和文本动作，生成反映动作效果的图像。 Fangjun Li,David C. Hogg,Anthony G. Cohn cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Alex Meiburg
title: Reduction Rules and ILP Are All You Need: Minimal Directed Feedback Vertex Set
title_zh: 您只需要归约规则和 ILP：最小定向反馈顶点集
abstract: This note describes the development of an exact solver for Minimal Directed Feedback Vertex Set as part of the PACE 2022 competition. The solver is powered largely by aggressively trying to reduce the DFVS problem to a Minimal Cover problem, and applying reduction rules adapted from Vertex Cover literature. The resulting problem is solved as an Integer Linear Program (ILP) using SCIP. The resulting solver performed the second-best in the competition, although a bug at submission time disqualified it. As an additional note, we describe a new vertex cover reduction generalizing the Desk reduction rule.
summary: This note describes the development of an exact solver for Minimal Directed Feedback Vertex Set as part of the PACE 2022 competition. The solver is powered largely by aggressively trying to reduce the DFVS problem to a Minimal Cover problem, and applying reduction rules adapted from Vertex Cover literature. The resulting problem is solved as an Integer Linear Program (ILP) using SCIP. The resulting solver performed the second-best in the competition, although a bug at submission time disqualified it. As an additional note, we describe a new vertex cover reduction generalizing the Desk reduction rule.
abstract_zh: 本说明描述了作为 PACE 2022 竞赛的一部分的最小定向反馈顶点集精确求解器的开发。该求解器的主要动力来自于积极尝试将 DFVS 问题简化为最小覆盖问题，并应用改编自顶点覆盖文献的简化规则。由此产生的问题可以使用 SCIP 作为整数线性规划 (ILP) 来解决。最终的求解器在比赛中表现第二好，尽管提交时的一个错误使其失去了参赛资格。作为补充说明，我们描述了一种新的顶点覆盖减少，概括了桌面减少规则。

AI.tldr: 开发用于解决最小有向反馈顶点集问题的精确求解器，作为PACE 2022竞赛的一部分。
AI.motivation: 通过应用简化规则和整数线性规划（ILP）方法，提高最小有向反馈顶点集问题的求解效率。
AI.method: 通过将DFVS问题转化为最小覆盖问题，应用顶点覆盖的简化规则，并使用SCIP求解器进行整数线性规划求解。
AI.result: 求解器在PACE 2022竞赛中排名第二，但因提交时的错误被取消资格，并提出了一种新的顶点覆盖简化规则。
AI.conclusion: 该方法展示了简化规则与ILP结合的有效性，尽管存在提交错误，但新规则为相关领域提供了有价值的参考。

authors_text: Jayeeta Mondal,Jeet Dutta,Hrishav Bakul Barua
title: VacciNet: Towards a Smart Framework for Learning the Distribution Chain Optimization of Vaccines for a Pandemic
title_zh: VacciNet：建立一个学习大流行疫苗分销链优化的智能框架
abstract: Vaccinations against viruses have always been the need of the hour since long past. However, it is hard to efficiently distribute the vaccines (on time) to all the corners of a country, especially during a pandemic. Considering the vastness of the population, diversified communities, and demands of a smart society, it is an important task to optimize the vaccine distribution strategy in any country/state effectively. Although there is a profusion of data (Big Data) from various vaccine administration sites that can be mined to gain valuable insights about mass vaccination drives, very few attempts has been made towards revolutionizing the traditional mass vaccination campaigns to mitigate the socio-economic crises of pandemic afflicted countries. In this paper, we bridge this gap in studies and experimentation. We collect daily vaccination data which is publicly available and carefully analyze it to generate meaning-full insights and predictions. We put forward a novel framework leveraging Supervised Learning and Reinforcement Learning (RL) which we call VacciNet, that is capable of learning to predict the demand of vaccination in a state of a country as well as suggest optimal vaccine allocation in the state for minimum cost of procurement and supply. At the present, our framework is trained and tested with vaccination data of the USA.
summary: Vaccinations against viruses have always been the need of the hour since long past. However, it is hard to efficiently distribute the vaccines (on time) to all the corners of a country, especially during a pandemic. Considering the vastness of the population, diversified communities, and demands of a smart society, it is an important task to optimize the vaccine distribution strategy in any country/state effectively. Although there is a profusion of data (Big Data) from various vaccine administration sites that can be mined to gain valuable insights about mass vaccination drives, very few attempts has been made towards revolutionizing the traditional mass vaccination campaigns to mitigate the socio-economic crises of pandemic afflicted countries. In this paper, we bridge this gap in studies and experimentation. We collect daily vaccination data which is publicly available and carefully analyze it to generate meaning-full insights and predictions. We put forward a novel framework leveraging Supervised Learning and Reinforcement Learning (RL) which we call VacciNet, that is capable of learning to predict the demand of vaccination in a state of a country as well as suggest optimal vaccine allocation in the state for minimum cost of procurement and supply. At the present, our framework is trained and tested with vaccination data of the USA.
abstract_zh: 自很久以前以来，针对病毒的疫苗一直是当前的需要。然而，很难将疫苗（按时）有效地分发到一个国家的各个角落，尤其是在大流行期间。考虑到人口众多、社区多元化以及智慧社会的需求，有效优化疫苗分配策略是任何国家/州的一项重要任务。尽管来自各个疫苗管理站点的大量数据（大数据）可以被挖掘以获得有关大规模疫苗接种活动的宝贵见解，但很少有人尝试彻底改变传统的大规模疫苗接种活动，以减轻受大流行影响的国家的社会经济危机。在本文中，我们弥合了研究和实验中的这一差距。我们收集公开的每日疫苗接种数据，并对其进行仔细分析，以生成有意义的见解和预测。我们提出了一种利用监督学习和强化学习（RL）的新颖框架，我们称之为 VacciNet，它能够学习预测一个国家某个州的疫苗接种需求，并建议该州以最低的采购和供应成本进行最佳疫苗分配。目前，我们的框架是用美国的疫苗接种数据进行训练和测试的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出VacciNet框架，结合监督学习和强化学习优化疫苗分发策略，以降低采购和供应成本。
AI.motivation: 疫情期间疫苗分发面临人口众多、需求多样化等挑战，现有研究未能有效利用大数据优化传统接种方案。
AI.method: 通过分析公开的每日接种数据，构建融合监督学习与强化学习的VacciNet框架，预测需求并优化分配方案。
AI.result: 框架在美国疫苗数据集上完成训练与测试，验证了其预测需求和成本优化的有效性。
AI.conclusion: VacciNet为疫情下的疫苗分发提供了智能化解决方案，有助于缓解社会经济危机。

authors_text: Ravi Pandya,Changliu Liu
title: Safe and Efficient Exploration of Human Models During Human-Robot Interaction
title_zh: 人机交互过程中安全高效的人体模型探索
abstract: Many collaborative human-robot tasks require the robot to stay safe and work efficiently around humans. Since the robot can only stay safe with respect to its own model of the human, we want the robot to learn a good model of the human in order to act both safely and efficiently. This paper studies methods that enable a robot to safely explore the space of a human-robot system to improve the robot's model of the human, which will consequently allow the robot to access a larger state space and better work with the human. In particular, we introduce active exploration under the framework of energy-function based safe control, investigate the effect of different active exploration strategies, and finally analyze the effect of safe active exploration on both analytical and neural network human models.
summary: Many collaborative human-robot tasks require the robot to stay safe and work efficiently around humans. Since the robot can only stay safe with respect to its own model of the human, we want the robot to learn a good model of the human in order to act both safely and efficiently. This paper studies methods that enable a robot to safely explore the space of a human-robot system to improve the robot's model of the human, which will consequently allow the robot to access a larger state space and better work with the human. In particular, we introduce active exploration under the framework of energy-function based safe control, investigate the effect of different active exploration strategies, and finally analyze the effect of safe active exploration on both analytical and neural network human models.
abstract_zh: 许多人机协作任务要求机器人在人类周围保持安全并高效工作。由于机器人只能在其自身的人类模型方面保持安全，因此我们希望机器人能够学习良好的人类模型，以便安全高效地行动。本文研究了使机器人能够安全地探索人机系统空间的方法，以改进机器人的人类模型，从而使机器人能够访问更大的状态空间并更好地与人类合作。特别是，我们在基于能量函数的安全控制框架下引入主动探索，研究不同主动探索策略的效果，最后分析安全主动探索对分析型和神经网络人体模型的影响。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在人机交互中通过安全高效的探索方法改进机器人对人类模型的学习，以提升协作安全性与效率。
AI.motivation: 机器人需基于自身的人类模型确保安全，但现有模型可能不准确，限制了状态空间的利用与协作效率。
AI.method: 提出基于能量函数的安全控制框架，结合主动探索策略，分析其对分析模型与神经网络模型的影响。
AI.result: 验证了安全主动探索策略能有效提升人类模型准确性，扩大机器人可操作状态空间并增强人机协作效率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保障安全的前提下优化了人类模型学习，为复杂人机协作场景提供了可行的探索框架。

ai_content.tldr: 本文研究了在人机交互中通过安全高效的探索方法改进机器人对人类模型的学习，以提升协作安全性与效率。

search_text: Safe and Efficient Exploration of Human Models During Human-Robot Interaction 人机交互过程中安全高效的人体模型探索 Many collaborative human-robot tasks require the robot to stay safe and work efficiently around humans. Since the robot can only stay safe with respect to its own model of the human, we want the robot to learn a good model of the human in order to act both safely and efficiently. This paper studies methods that enable a robot to safely explore the space of a human-robot system to improve the robot's model of the human, which will consequently allow the robot to access a larger state space and better work with the human. In particular, we introduce active exploration under the framework of energy-function based safe control, investigate the effect of different active exploration strategies, and finally analyze the effect of safe active exploration on both analytical and neural network human models. 许多人机协作任务要求机器人在人类周围保持安全并高效工作。由于机器人只能在其自身的人类模型方面保持安全，因此我们希望机器人能够学习良好的人类模型，以便安全高效地行动。本文研究了使机器人能够安全地探索人机系统空间的方法，以改进机器人的人类模型，从而使机器人能够访问更大的状态空间并更好地与人类合作。特别是，我们在基于能量函数的安全控制框架下引入主动探索，研究不同主动探索策略的效果，最后分析安全主动探索对分析型和神经网络人体模型的影响。 本文研究了在人机交互中通过安全高效的探索方法改进机器人对人类模型的学习，以提升协作安全性与效率。 Ravi Pandya,Changliu Liu cs.RO

authors_text: Jicheng Li,Anjana Bhat,Roghayeh Barmaki
title: Dyadic Movement Synchrony Estimation Under Privacy-preserving Conditions
title_zh: 隐私保护条件下的二元运动同步估计
abstract: Movement synchrony refers to the dynamic temporal connection between the motions of interacting people. The applications of movement synchrony are wide and broad. For example, as a measure of coordination between teammates, synchrony scores are often reported in sports. The autism community also identifies movement synchrony as a key indicator of children's social and developmental achievements. In general, raw video recordings are often used for movement synchrony estimation, with the drawback that they may reveal people's identities. Furthermore, such privacy concern also hinders data sharing, one major roadblock to a fair comparison between different approaches in autism research. To address the issue, this paper proposes an ensemble method for movement synchrony estimation, one of the first deep-learning-based methods for automatic movement synchrony assessment under privacy-preserving conditions. Our method relies entirely on publicly shareable, identity-agnostic secondary data, such as skeleton data and optical flow. We validate our method on two datasets: (1) PT13 dataset collected from autism therapy interventions and (2) TASD-2 dataset collected from synchronized diving competitions. In this context, our method outperforms its counterpart approaches, both deep neural networks and alternatives.
summary: Movement synchrony refers to the dynamic temporal connection between the motions of interacting people. The applications of movement synchrony are wide and broad. For example, as a measure of coordination between teammates, synchrony scores are often reported in sports. The autism community also identifies movement synchrony as a key indicator of children's social and developmental achievements. In general, raw video recordings are often used for movement synchrony estimation, with the drawback that they may reveal people's identities. Furthermore, such privacy concern also hinders data sharing, one major roadblock to a fair comparison between different approaches in autism research. To address the issue, this paper proposes an ensemble method for movement synchrony estimation, one of the first deep-learning-based methods for automatic movement synchrony assessment under privacy-preserving conditions. Our method relies entirely on publicly shareable, identity-agnostic secondary data, such as skeleton data and optical flow. We validate our method on two datasets: (1) PT13 dataset collected from autism therapy interventions and (2) TASD-2 dataset collected from synchronized diving competitions. In this context, our method outperforms its counterpart approaches, both deep neural networks and alternatives.
abstract_zh: 运动同步性是指交互的人的运动之间的动态时间联系。运动同步的应用非常广泛。例如，作为衡量队友之间协调性的指标，体育运动中经常报告同步得分。自闭症群体还将运动同步性视为儿童社交和发展成就的关键指标。一般来说，原始视频记录通常用于运动同步估计，其缺点是它们可能会泄露人们的身份。此外，这种隐私问题也阻碍了数据共享，这是自闭症研究中不同方法之间进行公平比较的主要障碍。为了解决这个问题，本文提出了一种用于运动同步估计的集成方法，这是第一个基于深度学习的在隐私保护条件下自动运动同步评估的方法之一。我们的方法完全依赖于可公开共享的、与身份无关的辅助数据，例如骨架数据和光流。我们在两个数据集上验证了我们的方法：（1）从自闭症治疗干预中收集的 PT13 数据集和（2）从同步跳水比赛中收集的 TASD-2 数据集。在这种情况下，我们的方法优于其对应方法，无论是深度神经网络还是替代方法。

AI.tldr: 提出一种在隐私保护条件下估计双人运动同步性的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统方法使用原始视频可能导致隐私泄露，阻碍数据共享和方法比较。
AI.method: 基于深度学习的集成方法，使用骨架数据和光流等隐私友好的数据。
AI.result: 在两个数据集上优于现有方法，包括深度神经网络和其他替代方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在隐私保护下有效评估运动同步性，促进数据共享与研究。

authors_text: Sina Shahhosseini,Yang Ni,Hamidreza Alikhani,Emad Kasaeyan Naeini,Mohsen Imani,Nikil Dutt,Amir M. Rahmani
title: Efficient Personalized Learning for Wearable Health Applications using HyperDimensional Computing
title_zh: 使用超维计算实现可穿戴健康应用的高效个性化学习
abstract: Health monitoring applications increasingly rely on machine learning techniques to learn end-user physiological and behavioral patterns in everyday settings. Considering the significant role of wearable devices in monitoring human body parameters, on-device learning can be utilized to build personalized models for behavioral and physiological patterns, and provide data privacy for users at the same time. However, resource constraints on most of these wearable devices prevent the ability to perform online learning on them. To address this issue, it is required to rethink the machine learning models from the algorithmic perspective to be suitable to run on wearable devices. Hyperdimensional computing (HDC) offers a well-suited on-device learning solution for resource-constrained devices and provides support for privacy-preserving personalization. Our HDC-based method offers flexibility, high efficiency, resilience, and performance while enabling on-device personalization and privacy protection. We evaluate the efficacy of our approach using three case studies and show that our system improves the energy efficiency of training by up to $45.8\times$ compared with the state-of-the-art Deep Neural Network (DNN) algorithms while offering a comparable accuracy.
summary: Health monitoring applications increasingly rely on machine learning techniques to learn end-user physiological and behavioral patterns in everyday settings. Considering the significant role of wearable devices in monitoring human body parameters, on-device learning can be utilized to build personalized models for behavioral and physiological patterns, and provide data privacy for users at the same time. However, resource constraints on most of these wearable devices prevent the ability to perform online learning on them. To address this issue, it is required to rethink the machine learning models from the algorithmic perspective to be suitable to run on wearable devices. Hyperdimensional computing (HDC) offers a well-suited on-device learning solution for resource-constrained devices and provides support for privacy-preserving personalization. Our HDC-based method offers flexibility, high efficiency, resilience, and performance while enabling on-device personalization and privacy protection. We evaluate the efficacy of our approach using three case studies and show that our system improves the energy efficiency of training by up to $45.8\times$ compared with the state-of-the-art Deep Neural Network (DNN) algorithms while offering a comparable accuracy.
abstract_zh: 健康监测应用程序越来越依赖机器学习技术来学习最终用户在日常环境中的生理和行为模式。考虑到可穿戴设备在监测人体参数方面的重要作用，设备端学习可用于构建行为和生理模式的个性化模型，同时为用户提供数据隐私。然而，大多数可穿戴设备的资源限制阻碍了在其上进行在线学习的能力。为了解决这个问题，需要从算法的角度重新思考机器学习模型，使其适合在可穿戴设备上运行。超维计算 (HDC) 为资源受限的设备提供了非常适合的设备端学习解决方案，并为保护隐私的个性化提供支持。我们基于 HDC 的方法提供灵活性、高效率、弹性和性能，同时实现设备上的个性化和隐私保护。我们使用三个案例研究评估了我们方法的有效性，结果表明，与最先进的深度神经网络 (DNN) 算法相比，我们的系统将训练的能源效率提高了高达 45.8 美元\倍$，同时提供了相当的精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于超维计算（HDC）的高效个性化学习方法，用于可穿戴健康应用中的设备端学习。
AI.motivation: 可穿戴设备资源受限，难以执行在线学习，需在算法层面重新设计模型以实现设备端个性化与隐私保护。
AI.method: 采用超维计算（HDC）框架，通过高维向量表示和简单运算实现高效、鲁棒的设备端学习。
AI.result: 相比最新深度神经网络（DNN）算法，系统训练能耗效率提升最高达45.8倍，且保持相近准确率。
AI.conclusion: HDC为资源受限设备提供了可行的隐私保护个性化学习方案，适用于可穿戴健康应用。

authors_text: Andrea Álvarez Pérez,Ana Iglesias-Molina,Lucía Prieto Santamaría,María Poveda-Villalón,Carlos Badenes-Olmedo,Alejandro Rodríguez-González
title: EBOCA: Evidences for BiOmedical Concepts Association Ontology
title_zh: EBOCA：生物医学概念关联本体论的证据
abstract: There is a large number of online documents data sources available nowadays. The lack of structure and the differences between formats are the main difficulties to automatically extract information from them, which also has a negative impact on its use and reuse. In the biomedical domain, the DISNET platform emerged to provide researchers with a resource to obtain information in the scope of human disease networks by means of large-scale heterogeneous sources. Specifically in this domain, it is critical to offer not only the information extracted from different sources, but also the evidence that supports it. This paper proposes EBOCA, an ontology that describes (i) biomedical domain concepts and associations between them, and (ii) evidences supporting these associations; with the objective of providing an schema to improve the publication and description of evidences and biomedical associations in this domain. The ontology has been successfully evaluated to ensure there are no errors, modelling pitfalls and that it meets the previously defined functional requirements. Test data coming from a subset of DISNET and automatic association extractions from texts has been transformed according to the proposed ontology to create a Knowledge Graph that can be used in real scenarios, and which has also been used for the evaluation of the presented ontology.
summary: There is a large number of online documents data sources available nowadays. The lack of structure and the differences between formats are the main difficulties to automatically extract information from them, which also has a negative impact on its use and reuse. This paper proposes EBOCA, an ontology that describes (i) biomedical domain concepts and associations between them, and (ii) evidences supporting these associations; with the objective of providing an schema to improve the publication and description of evidences and biomedical associations in this domain. The ontology has been successfully evaluated to ensure there are no errors, modelling pitfalls and that it meets the previously defined functional requirements. Test data coming from a subset of DISNET and automatic association extractions from texts has been transformed according to the proposed ontology to create a Knowledge Graph that can be used in real scenarios, and which has also been used for the evaluation of the presented ontology.
abstract_zh: 如今有大量可用的在线文档数据源。缺乏结构和格式之间的差异是自动​​从中提取信息的主要困难，这也对其使用和重用产生了负面影响。在生物医学领域，DISNET平台的出现，为研究人员提供了通过大规模异构源获取人类疾病网络范围内的信息的资源。特别是在这个领域，不仅提供从不同来源提取的信息，而且提供支持它的证据也至关重要。本文提出了 EBOCA，这是一种本体论，它描述了（i）生物医学领域概念及其之间的关联，以及（ii）支持这些关联的证据；目的是提供一个模式来改进该领域证据和生物医学关联的发布和描述。本体已被成功评估，以确保不存在错误、建模陷阱，并且满足先前定义的功能要求。来自 DISNET 子集和文本自动关联提取的测试数据已根据所提出的本体进行转换，以创建可在实际场景中使用的知识图，并且也用于评估所提出的本体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EBOCA本体，用于结构化生物医学概念关联及其证据，促进信息共享与重用。
AI.motivation: 现有生物医学数据源结构不统一，自动信息提取困难，且缺乏对关联证据的有效描述，影响数据的使用与重用。
AI.method: 设计EBOCA本体以描述生物医学概念、关联及支持证据，通过DISNET数据子集和文本自动关联提取构建知识图谱进行验证。
AI.result: EBOCA本体通过评估无误且满足需求，成功生成可用于实际场景的知识图谱，并验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: EBOCA为生物医学领域提供了结构化描述关联及证据的框架，有效支持信息的发布与知识图谱构建。

search_text: EBOCA: Evidences for BiOmedical Concepts Association Ontology EBOCA：生物医学概念关联本体论的证据 There is a large number of online documents data sources available nowadays. The lack of structure and the differences between formats are the main difficulties to automatically extract information from them, which also has a negative impact on its use and reuse. In the biomedical domain, the DISNET platform emerged to provide researchers with a resource to obtain information in the scope of human disease networks by means of large-scale heterogeneous sources. Specifically in this domain, it is critical to offer not only the information extracted from different sources, but also the evidence that supports it. This paper proposes EBOCA, an ontology that describes (i) biomedical domain concepts and associations between them, and (ii) evidences supporting these associations; with the objective of providing an schema to improve the publication and description of evidences and biomedical associations in this domain. The ontology has been successfully evaluated to ensure there are no errors, modelling pitfalls and that it meets the previously defined functional requirements. Test data coming from a subset of DISNET and automatic association extractions from texts has been transformed according to the proposed ontology to create a Knowledge Graph that can be used in real scenarios, and which has also been used for the evaluation of the presented ontology. 如今有大量可用的在线文档数据源。缺乏结构和格式之间的差异是自动​​从中提取信息的主要困难，这也对其使用和重用产生了负面影响。在生物医学领域，DISNET平台的出现，为研究人员提供了通过大规模异构源获取人类疾病网络范围内的信息的资源。特别是在这个领域，不仅提供从不同来源提取的信息，而且提供支持它的证据也至关重要。本文提出了 EBOCA，这是一种本体论，它描述了（i）生物医学领域概念及其之间的关联，以及（ii）支持这些关联的证据；目的是提供一个模式来改进该领域证据和生物医学关联的发布和描述。本体已被成功评估，以确保不存在错误、建模陷阱，并且满足先前定义的功能要求。来自 DISNET 子集和文本自动关联提取的测试数据已根据所提出的本体进行转换，以创建可在实际场景中使用的知识图，并且也用于评估所提出的本体。 本文提出EBOCA本体，用于结构化生物医学概念关联及其证据，促进信息共享与重用。 Andrea Álvarez Pérez,Ana Iglesias-Molina,Lucía Prieto Santamaría,María Poveda-Villalón,Carlos Badenes-Olmedo,Alejandro Rodríguez-González cs.AI

authors_text: Seungchan Kim,Chen Wang,Bowen Li,Sebastian Scherer
title: Robotic Interestingness via Human-Informed Few-Shot Object Detection
title_zh: 通过人类知情的少样本物体检测来提高机器人的趣味性
abstract: Interestingness recognition is crucial for decision making in autonomous exploration for mobile robots. Previous methods proposed an unsupervised online learning approach that can adapt to environments and detect interesting scenes quickly, but lack the ability to adapt to human-informed interesting objects. To solve this problem, we introduce a human-interactive framework, AirInteraction, that can detect human-informed objects via few-shot online learning. To reduce the communication bandwidth, we first apply an online unsupervised learning algorithm on the unmanned vehicle for interestingness recognition and then only send the potential interesting scenes to a base-station for human inspection. The human operator is able to draw and provide bounding box annotations for particular interesting objects, which are sent back to the robot to detect similar objects via few-shot learning. Only using few human-labeled examples, the robot can learn novel interesting object categories during the mission and detect interesting scenes that contain the objects. We evaluate our method on various interesting scene recognition datasets. To the best of our knowledge, it is the first human-informed few-shot object detection framework for autonomous exploration.
summary: Interestingness recognition is crucial for decision making in autonomous exploration for mobile robots. Previous methods proposed an unsupervised online learning approach that can adapt to environments and detect interesting scenes quickly, but lack the ability to adapt to human-informed interesting objects. To solve this problem, we introduce a human-interactive framework, AirInteraction, that can detect human-informed objects via few-shot online learning. To reduce the communication bandwidth, we first apply an online unsupervised learning algorithm on the unmanned vehicle for interestingness recognition and then only send the potential interesting scenes to a base-station for human inspection. The human operator is able to draw and provide bounding box annotations for particular interesting objects, which are sent back to the robot to detect similar objects via few-shot learning. Only using few human-labeled examples, the robot can learn novel interesting object categories during the mission and detect interesting scenes that contain the objects. We evaluate our method on various interesting scene recognition datasets. To the best of our knowledge, it is the first human-informed few-shot object detection framework for autonomous exploration.
abstract_zh: 兴趣识别对于移动机器人自主探索的决策至关重要。先前的方法提出了一种无监督的在线学习方法，可以适应环境并快速检测有趣的场景，但缺乏适应人类已知的有趣对象的能力。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了一种人机交互框架 AirInteraction，它可以通过少量在线学习来检测人类知情的对象。为了减少通信带宽，我们首先在无人车上应用在线无监督学习算法进行兴趣识别，然后仅将潜在的有趣场景发送到基站供人工检查。人类操作员能够为特定有趣的物体绘制并提供边界框注释，这些注释被发送回机器人以通过几次学习来检测相似的物体。只需使用少量人类标记的示例，机器人就可以在任务期间学习新奇的有趣对象类别，并检测包含这些对象的有趣场景。我们在各种有趣的场景识别数据集上评估我们的方法。据我们所知，它是第一个用于自主探索的人类知情的小样本目标检测框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于人类反馈的少样本目标检测框架AirInteraction，用于提升机器人自主探索中的趣味性识别能力。
AI.motivation: 现有方法虽能快速适应环境检测有趣场景，但缺乏对人类指定兴趣对象的适应能力，难以满足复杂任务需求。
AI.method: 采用两阶段人机协作机制：无人车通过在线无监督学习筛选潜在兴趣场景并上传至基站，人类标注兴趣目标后反馈给机器人，通过少样本学习实现新类别的实时检测。
AI.result: 在多个兴趣场景识别数据集上验证了方法有效性，实现了基于少量人类标注样本的新型兴趣目标检测。
AI.conclusion: 首次提出结合人类反馈的少样本检测框架，显著提升了机器人在自主探索任务中对新型兴趣目标的识别能力。

authors_text: Alessandro Wollek,Theresa Willem,Michael Ingrisch,Bastian Sabel,Tobias Lasser
title: A knee cannot have lung disease: out-of-distribution detection with in-distribution voting using the medical example of chest X-ray classification
title_zh: 膝盖不可能患有肺部疾病：使用胸部 X 射线分类的医学示例进行分布外检测和分布内投票
abstract: To investigate the impact of OOD radiographs on existing chest X-ray classification models and to increase their robustness against OOD data. The study employed the commonly used chest X-ray classification model, CheXnet, trained on the chest X-ray 14 data set, and tested its robustness against OOD data using three public radiography data sets: IRMA, Bone Age, and MURA, and the ImageNet data set. To detect OOD data for multi-label classification, we proposed in-distribution voting (IDV). The OOD detection performance is measured across data sets using the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) analysis and compared with Mahalanobis-based OOD detection, MaxLogit, MaxEnergy and self-supervised OOD detection (SS OOD). Without additional OOD detection, the chest X-ray classifier failed to discard any OOD images, with an AUC of 0.5. The proposed IDV approach trained on ID (chest X-ray 14) and OOD data (IRMA and ImageNet) achieved, on average, 0.999 OOD AUC across the three data sets, surpassing all other OOD detection methods. Mahalanobis-based OOD detection achieved an average OOD detection AUC of 0.982. IDV trained solely with a few thousand ImageNet images had an AUC 0.913, which was higher than MaxLogit (0.726), MaxEnergy (0.724), and SS OOD (0.476). The performance of all tested OOD detection methods did not translate well to radiography data sets, except Mahalanobis-based OOD detection and the proposed IDV method. Training solely on ID data led to incorrect classification of OOD images as ID, resulting in increased false positive rates. IDV substantially improved the model's ID classification performance, even when trained with data that will not occur in the intended use case or test set, without additional inference overhead.
summary: To investigate the impact of OOD radiographs on existing chest X-ray classification models and to increase their robustness against OOD data. The study employed the commonly used chest X-ray classification model, CheXnet, trained on the chest X-ray 14 data set, and tested its robustness against OOD data using three public radiography data sets: IRMA, Bone Age, and MURA, and the ImageNet data set. To detect OOD data for multi-label classification, we proposed in-distribution voting (IDV). The OOD detection performance is measured across data sets using the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) analysis and compared with Mahalanobis-based OOD detection, MaxLogit, MaxEnergy and self-supervised OOD detection (SS OOD). Without additional OOD detection, the chest X-ray classifier failed to discard any OOD images, with an AUC of 0.5. The proposed IDV approach trained on ID (chest X-ray 14) and OOD data (IRMA and ImageNet) achieved, on average, 0.999 OOD AUC across the three data sets, surpassing all other OOD detection methods. Mahalanobis-based OOD detection achieved an average OOD detection AUC of 0.982. IDV trained solely with a few thousand ImageNet images had an AUC 0.913, which was higher than MaxLogit (0.726), MaxEnergy (0.724), and SS OOD (0.476). The performance of all tested OOD detection methods did not translate well to radiography data sets, except Mahalanobis-based OOD detection and the proposed IDV method. Training solely on ID data led to incorrect classification of OOD images as ID, resulting in increased false positive rates. IDV substantially improved the model's ID classification performance, even when trained with data that will not occur in the intended use case or test set, without additional inference overhead.
abstract_zh: 研究 OOD 射线照片对现有胸部 X 射线分类模型的影响，并提高其针对 OOD 数据的鲁棒性。该研究采用了常用的胸部 X 光分类模型 CheXnet，在胸部 X 光 14 数据集上进行训练，并使用三个公共放射摄影数据集：IRMA、骨龄和 MURA 以及 ImageNet 数据集测试了其针对 OOD 数据的鲁棒性。为了检测多标签分类的 OOD 数据，我们提出了分布投票（IDV）。 OOD 检测性能是使用接受者操作特征曲线 (AUC) 分析下的面积跨数据集进行测量，并与基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测、MaxLogit、MaxEnergy 和自监督 OOD 检测 (SS OOD) 进行比较。如果没有额外的 OOD 检测，胸部 X 射线分类器无法丢弃任何 OOD 图像，AUC 为 0.5。所提出的 IDV 方法在 ID（胸部 X 射线 14）和 OOD 数据（IRMA 和 ImageNet）上进行训练，在三个数据集上平均实现了 0.999 OOD AUC，超过了所有其他 OOD 检测方法。基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测实现了 0.982 的平均 OOD 检测 AUC。仅使用数千张 ImageNet 图像训练的 IDV 的 AUC 为 0.913，高于 MaxLogit (0.726)、MaxEnergy (0.724) 和 SS OOD (0.476)。除了基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测和提出的 IDV 方法外，所有测试的 OOD 检测方法的性能都不能很好地转化为放射线照相数据集。仅针对 ID 数据进行训练会导致将 OOD 图像错误地分类为 ID，从而导致误报率增加。 IDV 极大地提高了模型的 ID 分类性能，即使使用不会出现在预期用例或测试集中的数据进行训练，也不会产生额外的推理开销。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了如何通过引入分布内投票（IDV）方法提升胸部X光分类模型对分布外（OOD）数据的检测能力，以减少误诊风险。
AI.motivation: 现有胸部X光分类模型在面对分布外数据（如膝关节X光片）时易产生错误分类，可能导致医疗误判，需提高模型对OOD数据的鲁棒性。
AI.method: 基于CheXnet模型，提出IDV方法，通过结合分布内（ID）数据与分布外（OOD）数据训练，利用多标签分类的投票机制检测OOD数据，并与Mahalanobis距离、MaxLogit等方法对比。
AI.result: IDV方法在三个数据集上平均达到0.999的AUC值，显著优于其他方法；仅用少量ImageNet图像训练的IDV仍取得0.913的AUC，而其他方法均低于0.726。
AI.conclusion: IDV方法有效提升了模型对OOD数据的检测能力，且无需额外推理开销，为医疗影像分类中的异常数据检测提供了实用方案。

search_text: A knee cannot have lung disease: out-of-distribution detection with in-distribution voting using the medical example of chest X-ray classification 膝盖不可能患有肺部疾病：使用胸部 X 射线分类的医学示例进行分布外检测和分布内投票 To investigate the impact of OOD radiographs on existing chest X-ray classification models and to increase their robustness against OOD data. The study employed the commonly used chest X-ray classification model, CheXnet, trained on the chest X-ray 14 data set, and tested its robustness against OOD data using three public radiography data sets: IRMA, Bone Age, and MURA, and the ImageNet data set. To detect OOD data for multi-label classification, we proposed in-distribution voting (IDV). The OOD detection performance is measured across data sets using the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) analysis and compared with Mahalanobis-based OOD detection, MaxLogit, MaxEnergy and self-supervised OOD detection (SS OOD). Without additional OOD detection, the chest X-ray classifier failed to discard any OOD images, with an AUC of 0.5. The proposed IDV approach trained on ID (chest X-ray 14) and OOD data (IRMA and ImageNet) achieved, on average, 0.999 OOD AUC across the three data sets, surpassing all other OOD detection methods. Mahalanobis-based OOD detection achieved an average OOD detection AUC of 0.982. IDV trained solely with a few thousand ImageNet images had an AUC 0.913, which was higher than MaxLogit (0.726), MaxEnergy (0.724), and SS OOD (0.476). The performance of all tested OOD detection methods did not translate well to radiography data sets, except Mahalanobis-based OOD detection and the proposed IDV method. Training solely on ID data led to incorrect classification of OOD images as ID, resulting in increased false positive rates. IDV substantially improved the model's ID classification performance, even when trained with data that will not occur in the intended use case or test set, without additional inference overhead. 研究 OOD 射线照片对现有胸部 X 射线分类模型的影响，并提高其针对 OOD 数据的鲁棒性。该研究采用了常用的胸部 X 光分类模型 CheXnet，在胸部 X 光 14 数据集上进行训练，并使用三个公共放射摄影数据集：IRMA、骨龄和 MURA 以及 ImageNet 数据集测试了其针对 OOD 数据的鲁棒性。为了检测多标签分类的 OOD 数据，我们提出了分布投票（IDV）。 OOD 检测性能是使用接受者操作特征曲线 (AUC) 分析下的面积跨数据集进行测量，并与基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测、MaxLogit、MaxEnergy 和自监督 OOD 检测 (SS OOD) 进行比较。如果没有额外的 OOD 检测，胸部 X 射线分类器无法丢弃任何 OOD 图像，AUC 为 0.5。所提出的 IDV 方法在 ID（胸部 X 射线 14）和 OOD 数据（IRMA 和 ImageNet）上进行训练，在三个数据集上平均实现了 0.999 OOD AUC，超过了所有其他 OOD 检测方法。基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测实现了 0.982 的平均 OOD 检测 AUC。仅使用数千张 ImageNet 图像训练的 IDV 的 AUC 为 0.913，高于 MaxLogit (0.726)、MaxEnergy (0.724) 和 SS OOD (0.476)。除了基于 Mahalanobis 的 OOD 检测和提出的 IDV 方法外，所有测试的 OOD 检测方法的性能都不能很好地转化为放射线照相数据集。仅针对 ID 数据进行训练会导致将 OOD 图像错误地分类为 ID，从而导致误报率增加。 IDV 极大地提高了模型的 ID 分类性能，即使使用不会出现在预期用例或测试集中的数据进行训练，也不会产生额外的推理开销。 本文研究了如何通过引入分布内投票（IDV）方法提升胸部X光分类模型对分布外（OOD）数据的检测能力，以减少误诊风险。 Alessandro Wollek,Theresa Willem,Michael Ingrisch,Bastian Sabel,Tobias Lasser eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Lingbo Li,Tianle Li,Xinyi He,Mengyu Zhou,Shi Han,Dongmei Zhang
title: ASTA: Learning Analytical Semantics over Tables for Intelligent Data Analysis and Visualization
title_zh: ASTA：通过表格学习分析语义以实现智能数据分析和可视化
abstract: Intelligent analysis and visualization of tables use techniques to automatically recommend useful knowledge from data, thus freeing users from tedious multi-dimension data mining. While many studies have succeeded in automating recommendations through rules or machine learning, it is difficult to generalize expert knowledge and provide explainable recommendations. In this paper, we present the recommendation of conditional formatting for the first time, together with chart recommendation, to exemplify intelligent table analysis. We propose analytical semantics over tables to uncover common analysis pattern behind user-created analyses. Here, we design analytical semantics by separating data focus from user intent, which extract the user motivation from data and human perspective respectively. Furthermore, the ASTA framework is designed by us to apply analytical semantics to multiple automated recommendations. ASTA framework extracts data features by designing signatures based on expert knowledge, and enables data referencing at field- (chart) or cell-level (conditional formatting) with pre-trained models. Experiments show that our framework achieves recall at top 1 of 62.86% on public chart corpora, outperforming the best baseline about 14%, and achieves 72.31% on the collected corpus ConFormT, validating that ASTA framework is effective in providing accurate and explainable recommendations.
summary: Intelligent analysis and visualization of tables use techniques to automatically recommend useful knowledge from data, thus freeing users from tedious multi-dimension data mining. While many studies have succeeded in automating recommendations through rules or machine learning, it is difficult to generalize expert knowledge and provide explainable recommendations. In this paper, we present the recommendation of conditional formatting for the first time, together with chart recommendation, to exemplify intelligent table analysis. We propose analytical semantics over tables to uncover common analysis pattern behind user-created analyses. Here, we design analytical semantics by separating data focus from user intent, which extract the user motivation from data and human perspective respectively. Furthermore, the ASTA framework is designed by us to apply analytical semantics to multiple automated recommendations. ASTA framework extracts data features by designing signatures based on expert knowledge, and enables data referencing at field- (chart) or cell-level (conditional formatting) with pre-trained models. Experiments show that our framework achieves recall at top 1 of 62.86% on public chart corpora, outperforming the best baseline about 14%, and achieves 72.31% on the collected corpus ConFormT, validating that ASTA framework is effective in providing accurate and explainable recommendations.
abstract_zh: 表格的智能分析和可视化利用技术自动从数据中推荐有用的知识，从而使用户从繁琐的多维度数据挖掘中解放出来。尽管许多研究已经成功地通过规则或机器学习实现了自动化推荐，但很难概括专家知识并提供可解释的建议。在本文中，我们首次提出条件格式的推荐以及图表推荐，以举例说明智能表格分析。我们提出了表的分析语义，以揭示用户创建的分析背后的常见分析模式。在这里，我们通过将数据焦点与用户意图分离来设计分析语义，分别从数据和人类角度提取用户动机。此外，我们设计的 ASTA 框架旨在将分析语义应用于多种自动推荐。 ASTA框架通过基于专家知识设计签名来提取数据特征，并通过预先训练的模型实现字段（图表）或单元格级别（条件格式化）的数据引用。实验表明，我们的框架在公共图表语料库上实现了 62.86% 的前 1 召回率，比最佳基线高出约 14%，并在收集的语料库 ConFormT 上达到了 72.31%，验证了 ASTA 框架可以有效地提供准确且可解释的推荐。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ASTA框架，通过学习表格的分析语义实现智能数据可视化与分析，包括条件格式推荐和图表推荐。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以泛化专家知识并提供可解释的推荐，需解决用户意图与数据特征分离的问题以提升推荐可解释性。
AI.method: 设计分析语义分离数据焦点与用户意图，构建ASTA框架利用专家知识设计特征签名，并通过预训练模型实现字段级（图表）和单元格级（条件格式）的数据关联。
AI.result: 在公开图表语料库中达到62.86%的Top-1召回率（优于最佳基线14%），在自建语料库ConFormT上达到72.31%。
AI.conclusion: ASTA框架有效实现了准确且可解释的智能表格分析推荐，验证了分析语义方法的有效性。

authors_text: Arushi,Roberto Dillon,Ai Ni Teoh,Denise Dillon
title: Voice Analysis for Stress Detection and Application in Virtual Reality to Improve Public Speaking in Real-time: A Review
title_zh: 用于压力检测的语音分析及其在虚拟现实中的应用以改善实时公共演讲：综述
abstract: Stress during public speaking is common and adversely affects performance and self-confidence. Extensive research has been carried out to develop various models to recognize emotional states. However, minimal research has been conducted to detect stress during public speaking in real time using voice analysis. In this context, the current review showed that the application of algorithms was not properly explored and helped identify the main obstacles in creating a suitable testing environment while accounting for current complexities and limitations. In this paper, we present our main idea and propose a stress detection computational algorithmic model that could be integrated into a Virtual Reality (VR) application to create an intelligent virtual audience for improving public speaking skills. The developed model, when integrated with VR, will be able to detect excessive stress in real time by analysing voice features correlated to physiological parameters indicative of stress and help users gradually control excessive stress and improve public speaking performance
summary: Stress during public speaking is common and adversely affects performance and self-confidence. Extensive research has been carried out to develop various models to recognize emotional states. However, minimal research has been conducted to detect stress during public speaking in real time using voice analysis. In this context, the current review showed that the application of algorithms was not properly explored and helped identify the main obstacles in creating a suitable testing environment while accounting for current complexities and limitations. In this paper, we present our main idea and propose a stress detection computational algorithmic model that could be integrated into a Virtual Reality (VR) application to create an intelligent virtual audience for improving public speaking skills. The developed model, when integrated with VR, will be able to detect excessive stress in real time by analysing voice features correlated to physiological parameters indicative of stress and help users gradually control excessive stress and improve public speaking performance
abstract_zh: 公开演讲期间的压力很常见，会对表现和自信心产生不利影响。人们已经进行了广泛的研究来开发各种模型来识别情绪状态。然而，利用语音分析实时检测公开演讲期间压力的研究却很少。在这种背景下，当前的审查表明，算法的应用没有得到适当的探索，并有助于确定创建合适的测试环境的主要障碍，同时考虑到当前的复杂性和局限性。在本文中，我们提出了我们的主要想法，并提出了一种压力检测计算算法模型，该模型可以集成到虚拟现实（VR）应用程序中，以创建智能虚拟观众来提高公开演讲技能。该模型与VR结合后，将能够通过分析与压力生理参数相关的语音特征，实时检测过度压力，帮助用户逐步控制过度压力，提高公开演讲表现

AI.tldr: 本文综述了实时语音分析在公共演讲压力检测中的应用，并提出将计算模型整合到虚拟现实（VR）中以改善公共演讲技能。
AI.motivation: 公共演讲压力影响表现和自信，但现有研究缺乏实时语音分析检测压力并结合VR的应用，需解决算法和测试环境的不足。
AI.method: 通过综述现有算法，识别技术障碍，并提出一个基于语音特征与生理参数关联的实时压力检测计算模型，整合至VR应用中。
AI.result: 开发出可集成于VR的实时压力检测模型，通过分析语音特征帮助用户控制压力并提升公共演讲表现。
AI.conclusion: 本文为未来结合VR与实时压力检测技术提升公共演讲能力提供了理论框架和方向，需进一步验证模型有效性。

search_text: Voice Analysis for Stress Detection and Application in Virtual Reality to Improve Public Speaking in Real-time: A Review 用于压力检测的语音分析及其在虚拟现实中的应用以改善实时公共演讲：综述 Stress during public speaking is common and adversely affects performance and self-confidence. Extensive research has been carried out to develop various models to recognize emotional states. However, minimal research has been conducted to detect stress during public speaking in real time using voice analysis. In this context, the current review showed that the application of algorithms was not properly explored and helped identify the main obstacles in creating a suitable testing environment while accounting for current complexities and limitations. In this paper, we present our main idea and propose a stress detection computational algorithmic model that could be integrated into a Virtual Reality (VR) application to create an intelligent virtual audience for improving public speaking skills. The developed model, when integrated with VR, will be able to detect excessive stress in real time by analysing voice features correlated to physiological parameters indicative of stress and help users gradually control excessive stress and improve public speaking performance 公开演讲期间的压力很常见，会对表现和自信心产生不利影响。人们已经进行了广泛的研究来开发各种模型来识别情绪状态。然而，利用语音分析实时检测公开演讲期间压力的研究却很少。在这种背景下，当前的审查表明，算法的应用没有得到适当的探索，并有助于确定创建合适的测试环境的主要障碍，同时考虑到当前的复杂性和局限性。在本文中，我们提出了我们的主要想法，并提出了一种压力检测计算算法模型，该模型可以集成到虚拟现实（VR）应用程序中，以创建智能虚拟观众来提高公开演讲技能。该模型与VR结合后，将能够通过分析与压力生理参数相关的语音特征，实时检测过度压力，帮助用户逐步控制过度压力，提高公开演讲表现 本文综述了实时语音分析在公共演讲压力检测中的应用，并提出将计算模型整合到虚拟现实（VR）中以改善公共演讲技能。 Arushi,Roberto Dillon,Ai Ni Teoh,Denise Dillon eess.AS,cs.HC,cs.LG,cs.MM,cs.SD

authors_text: Alex Wilf,Martin Q. Ma,Paul Pu Liang,Amir Zadeh,Louis-Philippe Morency
title: Face-to-Face Contrastive Learning for Social Intelligence Question-Answering
title_zh: 社交智能问答的面对面对比学习
abstract: Creating artificial social intelligence - algorithms that can understand the nuances of multi-person interactions - is an exciting and emerging challenge in processing facial expressions and gestures from multimodal videos. Recent multimodal methods have set the state of the art on many tasks, but have difficulty modeling the complex face-to-face conversational dynamics across speaking turns in social interaction, particularly in a self-supervised setup. In this paper, we propose Face-to-Face Contrastive Learning (F2F-CL), a graph neural network designed to model social interactions using factorization nodes to contextualize the multimodal face-to-face interaction along the boundaries of the speaking turn. With the F2F-CL model, we propose to perform contrastive learning between the factorization nodes of different speaking turns within the same video. We experimentally evaluated the challenging Social-IQ dataset and show state-of-the-art results.
summary: Creating artificial social intelligence - algorithms that can understand the nuances of multi-person interactions - is an exciting and emerging challenge in processing facial expressions and gestures from multimodal videos. Recent multimodal methods have set the state of the art on many tasks, but have difficulty modeling the complex face-to-face conversational dynamics across speaking turns in social interaction, particularly in a self-supervised setup. In this paper, we propose Face-to-Face Contrastive Learning (F2F-CL), a graph neural network designed to model social interactions using factorization nodes to contextualize the multimodal face-to-face interaction along the boundaries of the speaking turn. With the F2F-CL model, we propose to perform contrastive learning between the factorization nodes of different speaking turns within the same video. We experimentally evaluated the challenging Social-IQ dataset and show state-of-the-art results.
abstract_zh: 创建人工智能社交智能（可以理解多人交互的细微差别的算法）是处理多模态视频中的面部表情和手势的一个令人兴奋的新兴挑战。最近的多模态方法在许多任务上都达到了最先进的水平，但难以对社交互动中说话轮流中复杂的面对面对话动态进行建模，特别是在自我监督的设置中。在本文中，我们提出了面对面对比学习（F2F-CL），这是一种图神经网络，旨在使用分解节点对社交互动进行建模，以沿着说话回合的边界将多模态面对面互动情境化。通过 F2F-CL 模型，我们建议在同一视频中不同说话轮次的分解节点之间进行对比学习。我们通过实验评估了具有挑战性的社交智商数据集并展示了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为F2F-CL的图神经网络方法，用于建模多轮对话中的面部交互动态，以提升社交智能问答任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在自监督设置下难以有效建模多轮对话中的复杂面对面交互动态，尤其在处理说话轮次边界时存在不足。
AI.method: 设计基于因子化节点的图神经网络（F2F-CL），通过对比学习不同说话轮次的因子化节点，捕捉多模态面部交互的上下文信息。
AI.result: 在Social-IQ数据集上实验验证，F2F-CL方法达到当时最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: F2F-CL通过建模说话轮次边界处的交互动态，有效提升了社交智能问答任务的准确性。

authors_text: Akshay Iyer,Clifford Lindsay,Hendrik Pretorius,Michael King
title: Learning to estimate a surrogate respiratory signal from cardiac motion by signal-to-signal translation
title_zh: 学习通过信号到信号的转换来估计心脏运动的替代呼吸信号
abstract: In this work, we develop a neural network-based method to convert a noisy motion signal generated from segmenting rebinned list-mode cardiac SPECT images, to that of a high-quality surrogate signal, such as those seen from external motion tracking systems (EMTs). This synthetic surrogate will be used as input to our pre-existing motion correction technique developed for EMT surrogate signals. In our method, we test two families of neural networks to translate noisy internal motion to external surrogate: 1) fully connected networks and 2) convolutional neural networks. Our dataset consists of cardiac perfusion SPECT acquisitions for which cardiac motion was estimated (input: center-of-count-mass - COM signals) in conjunction with a respiratory surrogate motion signal acquired using a commercial Vicon Motion Tracking System (GT: EMT signals). We obtained an average R-score of 0.76 between the predicted surrogate and the EMT signal. Our goal is to lay a foundation to guide the optimization of neural networks for respiratory motion correction from SPECT without the need for an EMT.
summary: In this work, we develop a neural network-based method to convert a noisy motion signal generated from segmenting rebinned list-mode cardiac SPECT images, to that of a high-quality surrogate signal, such as those seen from external motion tracking systems (EMTs). This synthetic surrogate will be used as input to our pre-existing motion correction technique developed for EMT surrogate signals. In our method, we test two families of neural networks to translate noisy internal motion to external surrogate: 1) fully connected networks and 2) convolutional neural networks. Our dataset consists of cardiac perfusion SPECT acquisitions for which cardiac motion was estimated (input: center-of-count-mass - COM signals) in conjunction with a respiratory surrogate motion signal acquired using a commercial Vicon Motion Tracking System (GT: EMT signals). We obtained an average R-score of 0.76 between the predicted surrogate and the EMT signal. Our goal is to lay a foundation to guide the optimization of neural networks for respiratory motion correction from SPECT without the need for an EMT.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们开发了一种基于神经网络的方法，将分割重组列表模式心脏 SPECT 图像生成的噪声运动信号转换为高质量替代信号，例如从外部运动跟踪系统 (EMT) 看到的信号。该合成替代信号将用作我们为 EMT 替代信号开发的现有运动校正技术的输入。在我们的方法中，我们测试了两个神经网络系列，以将嘈杂的内部运动转换为外部代理：1）全连接网络和2）卷积神经网络。我们的数据集由心脏灌注 SPECT 采集数据组成，其中估计心脏运动（输入：计数质心 - COM 信号），并结合使用商业 Vicon 运动跟踪系统采集的呼吸替代运动信号（GT：EMT 信号）。我们获得的预测替代信号和 EMT 信号之间的平均 R 分数为 0.76。我们的目标是奠定基础，指导神经网络的优化，以通过 SPECT 进行呼吸运动校正，而无需 EMT。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经网络的方法，将心脏SPECT图像分割产生的噪声运动信号转换为高质量的外部呼吸代理信号，以替代外部运动追踪系统（EMT）的信号。
AI.motivation: 现有运动校正技术依赖外部运动追踪系统（EMT）获取的呼吸信号，但EMT设备成本高且操作复杂。本文旨在通过合成代理信号，实现无需EMT的呼吸运动校正优化。
AI.method: 采用全连接网络和卷积神经网络两种模型，利用心脏灌注SPECT数据集（包含中心计数质量信号和Vicon系统获取的EMT信号）进行训练，将噪声内部运动信号翻译为外部代理信号。
AI.result: 预测的代理信号与EMT信号的平均R值达到0.76，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文为无需EMT的SPECT呼吸运动校正提供了神经网络优化的基础，证明了信号到信号翻译方法的可行性。
