authors_text: Naima Otberdout,Claudio Ferrari,Mohamed Daoudi,Stefano Berretti,Alberto Del Bimbo
title: Generating Multiple 4D Expression Transitions by Learning Face Landmark Trajectories
title_zh: 通过学习人脸地标轨迹生成多个 4D 表情过渡
comment: This preprint is an extension of CVPR 2022 paper arXiv:2105.07463
comments: This preprint is an extension of CVPR 2022 paper arXiv:2105.07463
abstract: In this work, we address the problem of 4D facial expressions generation. This is usually addressed by animating a neutral 3D face to reach an expression peak, and then get back to the neutral state. In the real world though, people show more complex expressions, and switch from one expression to another. We thus propose a new model that generates transitions between different expressions, and synthesizes long and composed 4D expressions. This involves three sub-problems: (i) modeling the temporal dynamics of expressions, (ii) learning transitions between them, and (iii) deforming a generic mesh. We propose to encode the temporal evolution of expressions using the motion of a set of 3D landmarks, that we learn to generate by training a manifold-valued GAN (Motion3DGAN). To allow the generation of composed expressions, this model accepts two labels encoding the starting and the ending expressions. The final sequence of meshes is generated by a Sparse2Dense mesh Decoder (S2D-Dec) that maps the landmark displacements to a dense, per-vertex displacement of a known mesh topology. By explicitly working with motion trajectories, the model is totally independent from the identity. Extensive experiments on five public datasets show that our proposed approach brings significant improvements with respect to previous solutions, while retaining good generalization to unseen data.
summary: In this work, we address the problem of 4D facial expressions generation. This is usually addressed by animating a neutral 3D face to reach an expression peak, and then get back to the neutral state. In the real world though, people show more complex expressions, and switch from one expression to another. We thus propose a new model that generates transitions between different expressions, and synthesizes long and composed 4D expressions. This involves three sub-problems: (i) modeling the temporal dynamics of expressions, (ii) learning transitions between them, and (iii) deforming a generic mesh. We propose to encode the temporal evolution of expressions using the motion of a set of 3D landmarks, that we learn to generate by training a manifold-valued GAN (Motion3DGAN). To allow the generation of composed expressions, this model accepts two labels encoding the starting and the ending expressions. The final sequence of meshes is generated by a Sparse2Dense mesh Decoder (S2D-Dec) that maps the landmark displacements to a dense, per-vertex displacement of a known mesh topology. By explicitly working with motion trajectories, the model is totally independent from the identity. Extensive experiments on five public datasets show that our proposed approach brings significant improvements with respect to previous solutions, while retaining good generalization to unseen data.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们解决了 4D 面部表情生成的问题。通常通过对中性 3D 脸部进行动画处理以达到表情峰值，然后返回到中性状态来解决此问题。但在现实世界中，人们会表现出更复杂的表情，并从一种表情切换到另一种表情。因此，我们提出了一种新模型，可以生成不同表达式之间的转换，并合成长且组合的 4D 表达式。这涉及三个子问题：（i）对表达式的时间动态进行建模，（ii）学习它们之间的转换，以及（iii）使通用网格变形。我们建议使用一组 3D 地标的运动对表达式的时间演化进行编码，我们通过训练流形值 GAN (Motion3DGAN) 来学习生成这些地标。为了允许生成组合表达式，该模型接受两个对开始和结束表达式进行编码的标签。最终的网格序列由 Sparse2Dense 网格解码器 (S2D-Dec) 生成，该解码器将地标位移映射到已知网格拓扑的密集、每顶点位移。通过明确地处理运动轨迹，模型完全独立于身份。对五个公共数据集的广泛实验表明，我们提出的方法相对于以前的解决方案带来了显着改进，同时保留了对未见数据的良好泛化性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过学习面部关键点轨迹生成多组4D表情过渡的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统方法仅生成单一表情的动画，而现实中的面部表情更复杂，需在不同表情间进行过渡。现有方法难以处理这种复杂性。
AI.method: 使用Motion3DGAN建模表情的时序动态，通过3D关键点运动生成过渡轨迹，并利用Sparse2Dense解码器将稀疏关键点位移映射为密集网格变形。
AI.result: 在五个公开数据集上显著优于现有方法，且对未见数据具有良好的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过显式建模运动轨迹，实现了与身份无关的复杂4D表情生成，有效解决了表情过渡问题。

authors_text: Apurv Choudhari,Ameya Ekbote,Prerona Chaudhuri
title: Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem Using Conventional and Approximation Method
title_zh: 使用传统方法和近似方法的有能力车辆路径问题
abstract: This paper attempts to solve the famous Vehicle Routing Problem by considering multiple constraints including capacitated vehicles, single depot, and distance using two approaches namely, cluster first and route the second algorithm and using integer linear programming. A set of nodes are provided as input to the system and a feasible route is generated as output, giving clusters of nodes and the route to be traveled within the cluster. For clustering the nodes, we have adopted the DBSCAN algorithm, and the routing is done using the approximation algorithm, Christofide's algorithm. The solution generated can be employed for solving real-life situations, like delivery systems consisting of various demand nodes.
summary: This paper attempts to solve the famous Vehicle Routing Problem by considering multiple constraints including capacitated vehicles, single depot, and distance using two approaches namely, cluster first and route the second algorithm and using integer linear programming. A set of nodes are provided as input to the system and a feasible route is generated as output, giving clusters of nodes and the route to be traveled within the cluster. For clustering the nodes, we have adopted the DBSCAN algorithm, and the routing is done using the approximation algorithm, Christofide's algorithm. The solution generated can be employed for solving real-life situations, like delivery systems consisting of various demand nodes.
abstract_zh: 本文尝试通过考虑包括容量车辆、单个车库和距离在内的多种约束，使用两种方法（即聚类第一和路线第二算法）以及整数线性规划来解决著名的车辆路线问题。提供一组节点作为系统的输入，并生成一条可行的路线作为输出，给出节点集群以及集群内要行驶的路线。为了对节点进行聚类，我们采用了 DBSCAN 算法，并使用近似算法 Christofide 算法来完成路由。生成的解决方案可用于解决现实生活中的情况，例如由各种需求节点组成的交付系统。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过聚类优先和路线规划两种方法解决带容量约束的车辆路径问题，以优化实际配送系统。
AI.motivation: 解决实际物流配送中多约束（如车辆容量、单个仓库、距离限制）下的路径优化需求，提升配送效率。
AI.method: 采用DBSCAN算法进行节点聚类，结合Christofides近似算法规划路径，同时使用整数线性规划方法求解。
AI.result: 生成可行的节点集群及路径方案，适用于实际配送场景中包含多个需求节点的物流系统。
AI.conclusion: 两种方法均能有效解决带容量约束的车辆路径问题，为实际物流配送提供可行的优化方案。

authors_text: Qingjie Meng,Chen Qin,Wenjia Bai,Tianrui Liu,Antonio de Marvao,Declan P O'Regan,Daniel Rueckert
title: MulViMotion: Shape-aware 3D Myocardial Motion Tracking from Multi-View Cardiac MRI
title_zh: MulViMotion：通过多视图心脏 MRI 进行形状感知 3D 心肌运动跟踪
abstract: Recovering the 3D motion of the heart from cine cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) imaging enables the assessment of regional myocardial function and is important for understanding and analyzing cardiovascular disease. However, 3D cardiac motion estimation is challenging because the acquired cine CMR images are usually 2D slices which limit the accurate estimation of through-plane motion. To address this problem, we propose a novel multi-view motion estimation network (MulViMotion), which integrates 2D cine CMR images acquired in short-axis and long-axis planes to learn a consistent 3D motion field of the heart. In the proposed method, a hybrid 2D/3D network is built to generate dense 3D motion fields by learning fused representations from multi-view images. To ensure that the motion estimation is consistent in 3D, a shape regularization module is introduced during training, where shape information from multi-view images is exploited to provide weak supervision to 3D motion estimation. We extensively evaluate the proposed method on 2D cine CMR images from 580 subjects of the UK Biobank study for 3D motion tracking of the left ventricular myocardium. Experimental results show that the proposed method quantitatively and qualitatively outperforms competing methods.
summary: Recovering the 3D motion of the heart from cine cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) imaging enables the assessment of regional myocardial function and is important for understanding and analyzing cardiovascular disease. However, 3D cardiac motion estimation is challenging because the acquired cine CMR images are usually 2D slices which limit the accurate estimation of through-plane motion. To address this problem, we propose a novel multi-view motion estimation network (MulViMotion), which integrates 2D cine CMR images acquired in short-axis and long-axis planes to learn a consistent 3D motion field of the heart. In the proposed method, a hybrid 2D/3D network is built to generate dense 3D motion fields by learning fused representations from multi-view images. To ensure that the motion estimation is consistent in 3D, a shape regularization module is introduced during training, where shape information from multi-view images is exploited to provide weak supervision to 3D motion estimation. We extensively evaluate the proposed method on 2D cine CMR images from 580 subjects of the UK Biobank study for 3D motion tracking of the left ventricular myocardium. Experimental results show that the proposed method quantitatively and qualitatively outperforms competing methods.
abstract_zh: 从电影心脏磁共振 (CMR) 成像中恢复心脏的 3D 运动可以评估局部心肌功能，对于理解和分析心血管疾病非常重要。然而，3D 心脏运动估计具有挑战性，因为获取的电影 CMR 图像通常是 2D 切片，这限制了平面运动的准确估计。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的多视图运动估计网络（MulViMotion），它集成了在短轴和长轴平面上获取的 2D 电影 CMR 图像，以学习心脏的一致 3D 运动场。在所提出的方法中，构建了混合 2D/3D 网络，通过学习多视图图像的融合表示来生成密集的 3D 运动场。为了确保运动估计在 3D 中保持一致，在训练期间引入了形状正则化模块，其中利用多视图图像的形状信息为 3D 运动估计提供弱监督。我们在来自英国生物银行研究的 580 名受试者的 2D 电影 CMR 图像上广泛评估了所提出的方法，用于左心室心肌的 3D 运动跟踪。实验结果表明，所提出的方法在数量和质量上都优于竞争方法。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于多视角心脏MRI图像的三维心肌运动追踪方法MulViMotion，解决传统二维切片图像难以准确估计通过平面运动的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统二维心脏MRI图像无法准确捕捉三维心肌运动，尤其在通过平面方向存在显著局限，需结合多视角信息提升运动估计精度。
AI.method: 构建混合2D/3D网络融合短轴与长轴图像生成三维运动场，并引入形状正则化模块利用多视角形状信息提供弱监督约束。
AI.result: 在UK Biobank的580例数据集上，定量指标和可视化结果均优于现有方法，验证了方法在三维心肌运动追踪中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: MulViMotion通过多视角信息融合与形状约束实现了更精确的三维心肌运动估计，为心血管疾病分析提供可靠工具。

authors_text: Alejo Nevado-Holgado,Colin Espie,Maria Liakata,Alasdair Henry,Jenny Gu,Niall Taylor,Kate Saunders,Tom Walker,Chris Miller
title: Personalised recommendations of sleep behaviour with neural networks using sleep diaries captured in Sleepio
title_zh: 使用 Sleepio 中捕获的睡眠日记，通过神经网络提供睡眠行为的个性化建议
abstract: SleepioTM is a digital mobile phone and web platform that uses techniques from cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) to improve sleep in people with sleep difficulty. As part of this process, Sleepio captures data about the sleep behaviour of the users that have consented to such data being processed. For neural networks, the scale of the data is an opportunity to train meaningful models translatable to actual clinical practice. In collaboration with Big Health, the therapeutics company that created and utilizes Sleepio, we have analysed data from a random sample of 401,174 sleep diaries and built a neural network to model sleep behaviour and sleep quality of each individual in a personalised manner. We demonstrate that this neural network is more accurate than standard statistical methods in predicting the sleep quality of an individual based on his/her behaviour from the last 10 days. We compare model performance in a wide range of hyperparameter settings representing various scenarios. We further show that the neural network can be used to produce personalised recommendations of what sleep habits users should follow to maximise sleep quality, and show that these recommendations are substantially better than the ones generated by standard methods. We finally show that the neural network can explain the recommendation given to each participant and calculate confidence intervals for each prediction, all of which are essential for clinicians to be able to adopt such a tool in clinical practice.
summary: SleepioTM is a digital mobile phone and web platform that uses techniques from cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) to improve sleep in people with sleep difficulty. As part of this process, Sleepio captures data about the sleep behaviour of the users that have consented to such data being processed. For neural networks, the scale of the data is an opportunity to train meaningful models translatable to actual clinical practice. In collaboration with Big Health, the therapeutics company that created and utilizes Sleepio, we have analysed data from a random sample of 401,174 sleep diaries and built a neural network to model sleep behaviour and sleep quality of each individual in a personalised manner. We demonstrate that this neural network is more accurate than standard statistical methods in predicting the sleep quality of an individual based on his/her behaviour from the last 10 days. We compare model performance in a wide range of hyperparameter settings representing various scenarios. We further show that the neural network can be used to produce personalised recommendations of what sleep habits users should follow to maximise sleep quality, and show that these recommendations are substantially better than the ones generated by standard methods. We finally show that the neural network can explain the recommendation given to each participant and calculate confidence intervals for each prediction, all of which are essential for clinicians to be able to adopt such a tool in clinical practice.
abstract_zh: SleepioTM 是一款数字手机和网络平台，利用认知行为疗法 (CBT) 技术来改善睡眠困难人群的睡眠。作为此过程的一部分，Sleepio 会捕获有关已同意处理此类数据的用户的睡眠行为的数据。对于神经网络来说，数据规模是训练可转化为实际临床实践的有意义模型的机会。我们与创建并使用 Sleepio 的治疗公司 Big Health 合作，分析了 401,174 份睡眠日记的随机样本数据，并建立了一个神经网络，以个性化的方式对每个人的睡眠行为和睡眠质量进行建模。我们证明，在根据个人过去 10 天的行为来预测个人的睡眠质量方面，该神经网络比标准统计方法更准确。我们比较了代表各种场景的各种超参数设置中的模型性能。我们进一步表明，神经网络可用于生成用户应遵循的睡眠习惯以最大限度地提高睡眠质量的个性化建议，并表明这些建议比标准方法生成的建议要好得多。我们最终证明，神经网络可以解释给每个参与者的建议，并计算每个预测的置信区间，所有这些对于临床医生能够在临床实践中采用这种工具至关重要。

AI.tldr: 本文利用Sleepio平台收集的睡眠日记数据，构建神经网络模型以个性化推荐睡眠行为，提升睡眠质量预测与建议的准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统统计方法在睡眠质量预测和个性化建议方面存在局限，而Sleepio平台的大规模睡眠数据为神经网络提供了训练机会，可推动临床实践应用。
AI.method: 基于401,174份睡眠日记数据，训练神经网络模型并对比多种超参数设置下的性能，验证其预测精度及推荐效果，同时计算置信区间与模型解释性。
AI.result: 神经网络在预测个体睡眠质量及生成个性化建议方面显著优于传统方法，且能提供可解释的推荐结果与置信区间，满足临床需求。
AI.conclusion: 该神经网络模型有效提升了睡眠行为推荐的准确性与可解释性，为临床实践提供了可靠工具。

authors_text: Briskline Kiruba S,Petchiammal A,D. Murugan
title: A review of Deep learning Techniques for COVID-19 identification on Chest CT images
title_zh: 胸部 CT 图像上的 COVID-19 识别深度学习技术综述
comment: Uploaded a different draft by mistake
comments: Uploaded a different draft by mistake
abstract: The current COVID-19 pandemic is a serious threat to humanity that directly affects the lungs. Automatic identification of COVID-19 is a challenge for health care officials. The standard gold method for diagnosing COVID-19 is Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) to collect swabs from affected people. Some limitations encountered while collecting swabs are related to accuracy and longtime duration. Chest CT (Computed Tomography) is another test method that helps healthcare providers quickly identify the infected lung areas. It was used as a supporting tool for identifying COVID-19 in an earlier stage. With the help of deep learning, the CT imaging characteristics of COVID-19. Researchers have proven it to be highly effective for COVID-19 CT image classification. In this study, we review the recent deep learning techniques that can use to detect the COVID-19 disease. Relevant studies were collected by various databases such as Web of Science, Google Scholar, and PubMed. Finally, we compare the results of different deep learning models, and CT image analysis is discussed.

Relevant studies were collected by various databases such as Web of Science, Google Scholar, and PubMed. Finally, we compare the results of different deep learning models, and CT image analysis is discussed.
abstract_zh: 当前的 COVID-19 大流行对人类构成严重威胁，直接影响肺部。自动识别 COVID-19 对卫生保健官员来说是一个挑战。诊断 COVID-19 的标准黄金方法是通过逆转录聚合酶链反应 (RT-PCR) 收集受影响人群的拭子。收集拭子时遇到的一些限制与准确性和长时间持续时间有关。胸部 CT（计算机断层扫描）是另一种测试方法，可帮助医疗保健提供者快速识别受感染的肺部区域。它被用作早期识别COVID-19的支持工具。借助深度学习，研究了COVID-19的CT成像特征。研究人员已经证明它对于 COVID-19 CT 图像分类非常有效。在这项研究中，我们回顾了最近可用于检测 COVID-19 疾病的深度学习技术。相关研究被各种数据库收集，例如 Web of Science、Google Scholar 和 PubMed。最后，我们比较了不同深度学习模型的结果，并讨论了 CT 图像分析。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了用于胸部CT图像中COVID-19识别的深度学习技术。
AI.motivation: 由于RT-PCR检测存在准确性低和耗时长的局限性，亟需通过胸部CT图像结合深度学习技术实现快速有效的COVID-19检测。
AI.method: 通过Web of Science、Google Scholar和PubMed等数据库收集相关研究，比较不同深度学习模型在CT图像分析中的表现。
AI.result: 深度学习方法在COVID-19 CT图像分类中表现出高有效性，不同模型的性能差异被系统比较。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习技术为COVID-19的早期识别提供了可靠工具，未来需进一步优化模型以提高检测精度和效率。

search_text: A review of Deep learning Techniques for COVID-19 identification on Chest CT images 胸部 CT 图像上的 COVID-19 识别深度学习技术综述 The current COVID-19 pandemic is a serious threat to humanity that directly affects the lungs. Automatic identification of COVID-19 is a challenge for health care officials. The standard gold method for diagnosing COVID-19 is Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) to collect swabs from affected people. Some limitations encountered while collecting swabs are related to accuracy and longtime duration. Chest CT (Computed Tomography) is another test method that helps healthcare providers quickly identify the infected lung areas. It was used as a supporting tool for identifying COVID-19 in an earlier stage. With the help of deep learning, the CT imaging characteristics of COVID-19. Researchers have proven it to be highly effective for COVID-19 CT image classification. In this study, we review the recent deep learning techniques that can use to detect the COVID-19 disease. Relevant studies were collected by various databases such as Web of Science, Google Scholar, and PubMed. Finally, we compare the results of different deep learning models, and CT image analysis is discussed. 当前的 COVID-19 大流行对人类构成严重威胁，直接影响肺部。自动识别 COVID-19 对卫生保健官员来说是一个挑战。诊断 COVID-19 的标准黄金方法是通过逆转录聚合酶链反应 (RT-PCR) 收集受影响人群的拭子。收集拭子时遇到的一些限制与准确性和长时间持续时间有关。胸部 CT（计算机断层扫描）是另一种测试方法，可帮助医疗保健提供者快速识别受感染的肺部区域。它被用作早期识别COVID-19的支持工具。借助深度学习，研究了COVID-19的CT成像特征。研究人员已经证明它对于 COVID-19 CT 图像分类非常有效。在这项研究中，我们回顾了最近可用于检测 COVID-19 疾病的深度学习技术。相关研究被各种数据库收集，例如 Web of Science、Google Scholar 和 PubMed。最后，我们比较了不同深度学习模型的结果，并讨论了 CT 图像分析。 本文综述了用于胸部CT图像中COVID-19识别的深度学习技术。 Briskline Kiruba S,Petchiammal A,D. authors_text: Petchiammal A,Briskline Kiruba S,D. Murugan
title: Paddy Leaf diseases identification on Infrared Images based on Convolutional Neural Networks
title_zh: 基于卷积神经网络的红外图像水稻叶部病害识别
comment: Uploaded a different draft by mistake
comments: Uploaded a different draft by mistake
abstract: Agriculture is the mainstay of human society because it is an essential need for every organism. Paddy cultivation is very significant so far as humans are concerned, largely in the Asian continent, and it is one of the staple foods. However, plant diseases in agriculture lead to depletion in productivity. Plant diseases are generally caused by pests, insects, and pathogens that decrease productivity to a large scale if not controlled within a particular time. Eventually, one cannot see an increase in paddy yield. Accurate and timely identification of plant diseases can help farmers mitigate losses due to pests and diseases. Recently, deep learning techniques have been used to identify paddy diseases and overcome these problems. This paper implements a convolutional neural network (CNN) based on a model and tests a public dataset consisting of 636 infrared image samples with five paddy disease classes and one healthy class. The proposed model proficiently identified and classified paddy diseases of five different types and achieved an accuracy of 88.28%

However, plant diseases in agriculture lead to depletion in productivity. Plant diseases are generally caused by pests, insects, and pathogens that decrease productivity to a large scale if not controlled within a particular time. Eventually, one cannot see an increase in paddy yield. Accurate and timely identification of plant diseases can help farmers mitigate losses due to pests and diseases. Recently, deep learning techniques have been used to identify paddy diseases and overcome these problems. This paper implements a convolutional neural network (CNN) based on a model and tests a public dataset consisting of 636 infrared image samples with five paddy disease classes and one healthy class. The proposed model proficiently identified and classified paddy diseases of five different types and achieved an accuracy of 88.28%
abstract_zh: 农业是人类社会的支柱，因为它是每个有机体的基本需求。水稻种植对于人类来说非常重要，主要在亚洲大陆，它是主食之一。然而，农业中的植物病害导致生产力下降。植物病害通常是由害虫、昆虫和病原体引起，如果不在特定时间内加以控制，就会导致生产力大幅下降。最终，人们看不到水稻产量的增加。准确及时地识别植物病害可以帮助农民减少病虫害造成的损失。最近，深度学习技术已被用于识别水稻病害并克服这些问题。本文基于模型实现了卷积神经网络（CNN），并测试了由 636 个红外图像样本组成的公共数据集，其中包含 5 个水稻病害类别和 1 个健康类别。该模型能够熟练地识别和分类五种不同类型的水稻病害，准确率达到 88.28%

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的模型，用于通过红外图像识别稻叶疾病，实现五种病害及健康叶片的分类。
AI.motivation: 稻米种植对亚洲农业至关重要，但病害会显著降低产量。传统方法难以及时准确识别病害，需借助深度学习技术提升检测效率。
AI.method: 构建CNN模型，使用包含636张红外图像的公开数据集（含5种病害类别和1种健康类别）进行训练与测试。
AI.result: 模型在测试集上达到88.28%的分类准确率，能有效识别五种稻叶病害。
AI.conclusion: CNN方法在红外图像病害识别中表现优异，为农业病害检测提供了高效解决方案，有助于减少农民损失。

authors_text: Colin Conwell,Tomer Ullman
title: Testing Relational Understanding in Text-Guided Image Generation
title_zh: 测试文本引导图像生成中的关系理解
abstract: Relations are basic building blocks of human cognition. Classic and recent work suggests that many relations are early developing, and quickly perceived. Machine models that aspire to human-level perception and reasoning should reflect the ability to recognize and reason generatively about relations. We report a systematic empirical examination of a recent text-guided image generation model (DALL-E 2), using a set of 15 basic physical and social relations studied or proposed in the literature, and judgements from human participants (N = 169). Overall, we find that only ~22% of images matched basic relation prompts. Based on a quantitative examination of people's judgments, we suggest that current image generation models do not yet have a grasp of even basic relations involving simple objects and agents. We examine reasons for model successes and failures, and suggest possible improvements based on computations observed in biological intelligence.
summary: Relations are basic building blocks of human cognition. Classic and recent work suggests that many relations are early developing, and quickly perceived. Machine models that aspire to human-level perception and reasoning should reflect the ability to recognize and reason generatively about relations. We report a systematic empirical examination of a recent text-guided image generation model (DALL-E 2), using a set of 15 basic physical and social relations studied or proposed in the literature, and judgements from human participants (N = 169). Overall, we find that only ~22% of images matched basic relation prompts. Based on a quantitative examination of people's judgments, we suggest that current image generation models do not yet have a grasp of even basic relations involving simple objects and agents. We examine reasons for model successes and failures, and suggest possible improvements based on computations observed in biological intelligence.
abstract_zh: 关系是人类认知的基本组成部分。经典和最近的研究表明，许多关系正在早期发展，并很快被感知。渴望达到人类水平的感知和推理的机器模型应该反映出对关系进行生成性识别和推理的能力。我们报告了对最近的文本引导图像生成模型 (DALL-E 2) 的系统实证检验，使用文献中研究或提出的 15 种基本物理和社会关系以及人类参与者的判断 (N = 169)。总体而言，我们发现只有约 22% 的图像符合基本关系提示。基于对人们判断的定量检查，我们认为当前的图像生成模型甚至还没有掌握涉及简单对象和代理的基本关系。我们研究模型成功和失败的原因，并根据生物智能中观察到的计算提出可能的改进建议。

AI.tldr: 该论文评估了文本引导图像生成模型（如DALL-E 2）在理解基本物理和社会关系方面的能力。
AI.motivation: 关系是人类认知的基础，但现有模型是否具备类似能力尚不明确，需通过系统性测试验证并推动模型改进。
AI.method: 通过15种基本关系的提示生成图像，收集169名参与者对生成图像与关系匹配度的判断，并进行定量分析。
AI.result: 仅约22%的生成图像能正确匹配基本关系提示，表明模型在理解简单物体和代理关系方面存在显著不足。
AI.conclusion: 当前图像生成模型尚未掌握基础关系理解能力，需借鉴生物智能的计算机制以提升关系推理性能。

We examine reasons for model successes and failures, and suggest possible improvements based on computations observed in biological intelligence. 关系是人类认知的基本组成部分。经典和最近的研究表明，许多关系正在早期发展，并很快被感知。渴望达到人类水平的感知和推理的机器模型应该反映出对关系进行生成性识别和推理的能力。我们报告了对最近的文本引导图像生成模型 (DALL-E 2) 的系统实证检验，使用文献中研究或提出的 15 种基本物理和社会关系以及人类参与者的判断 (N = 169)。总体而言，我们发现只有约 22% 的图像符合基本关系提示。基于对人们判断的定量检查，我们认为当前的图像生成模型甚至还没有掌握涉及简单对象和代理的基本关系。我们研究模型成功和失败的原因，并根据生物智能中观察到的计算提出可能的改进建议。 该论文评估了文本引导图像生成模型（如DALL-E 2）在理解基本物理和社会关系方面的能力。 Colin Conwell,Tomer Ullman cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Viktor Zobernig,Richard A. Saldanha,Jinke He,Erica van der Sar,Jasper van Doorn,Jia-Chen Hua,Lachlan R. Mason,Aleksander Czechowski,Drago Indjic,Tomasz Kosmala,Alessandro Zocca,Sandjai Bhulai,Jorge Montalvo Arvizu,Claude Klöckl,John Moriarty
title: RangL: A Reinforcement Learning Competition Platform
title_zh: RangL：强化学习竞赛平台
comment: Documents in general and premierly the RangL competition plattform and in particular its 2022's competition "Pathways to Netzero" 10 pages, 2 figures, 1 table, Comments welcome!
comments: Documents in general and premierly the RangL competition plattform and in particular its 2022's competition "Pathways to Netzero" 10 pages, 2 figures, 1 table, Comments welcome!
abstract: The RangL project hosted by The Alan Turing Institute aims to encourage the wider uptake of reinforcement learning by supporting competitions relating to real-world dynamic decision problems. This article describes the reusable code repository developed by the RangL team and deployed for the 2022 Pathways to Net Zero Challenge, supported by the UK Net Zero Technology Centre. The winning solutions to this particular Challenge seek to optimize the UK's energy transition policy to net zero carbon emissions by 2050. The RangL repository includes an OpenAI Gym reinforcement learning environment and code that supports both submission to, and evaluation in, a remote instance of the open source EvalAI platform as well as all winning learning agent strategies. The repository is an illustrative example of RangL's capability to provide a reusable structure for future challenges.
summary: The RangL project hosted by The Alan Turing Institute aims to encourage the wider uptake of reinforcement learning by supporting competitions relating to real-world dynamic decision problems. This article describes the reusable code repository developed by the RangL team and deployed for the 2022 Pathways to Net Zero Challenge, supported by the UK Net Zero Technology Centre. The winning solutions to this particular Challenge seek to optimize the UK's energy transition policy to net zero carbon emissions by 2050. The RangL repository includes an OpenAI Gym reinforcement learning environment and code that supports both submission to, and evaluation in, a remote instance of the open source EvalAI platform as well as all winning learning agent strategies. The repository is an illustrative example of RangL's capability to provide a reusable structure for future challenges.
abstract_zh: 由艾伦图灵研究所主办的 RangL 项目旨在通过支持与现实世界动态决策问题相关的竞赛来鼓励更广泛地采用强化学习。本文介绍了由 RangL 团队开发并为英国净零技术中心支持的 2022 年净零挑战之路部署的可重用代码存储库。这一特定挑战赛的获胜解决方案旨在优化英国的能源转型政策，到 2050 年实现净零碳排放。RangL 存储库包括 OpenAI Gym 强化学习环境和代码，支持向开源 EvalAI 平台的远程实例以及所有获胜学习代理策略提交和评估。该存储库是 RangL 为未来挑战提供可重用结构能力的说明性示例。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了RangL项目开发的可复用代码库，用于举办强化学习竞赛，特别是支持英国净零碳排放挑战赛。
AI.motivation: 通过竞赛推动强化学习在现实世界动态决策问题中的应用，如优化能源转型政策以实现碳中和目标。
AI.method: 构建包含OpenAI Gym环境和EvalAI平台对接代码的代码库，支持竞赛提交、评估及获胜策略的复用。
AI.result: 成功部署代码库并应用于2022年净零挑战赛，展示了RangL平台为未来竞赛提供标准化框架的能力。
AI.conclusion: RangL平台通过可复用的竞赛框架促进了强化学习研究与实际问题的结合，为后续挑战赛提供了范例。

authors_text: Zijue Chen,Keenan Granland,Rhys Newbury,Chao Chen
title: HOB-CNN: Hallucination of Occluded Branches with a Convolutional Neural Network for 2D Fruit Trees
title_zh: HOB-CNN：利用 2D 果树的卷积神经网络产生被遮挡树枝的幻觉
abstract: Orchard automation has attracted the attention of researchers recently due to the shortage of global labor force. To automate tasks in orchards such as pruning, thinning, and harvesting, a detailed understanding of the tree structure is required. However, occlusions from foliage and fruits can make it challenging to predict the position of occluded trunks and branches. This work proposes a regression-based deep learning model, Hallucination of Occluded Branch Convolutional Neural Network (HOB-CNN), for tree branch position prediction in varying occluded conditions. We formulate tree branch position prediction as a regression problem towards the horizontal locations of the branch along the vertical direction or vice versa. We present comparative experiments on Y-shaped trees with two state-of-the-art baselines, representing common approaches to the problem. Experiments show that HOB-CNN outperform the baselines at predicting branch position and shows robustness against varying levels of occlusion. We further validated HOB-CNN against two different types of 2D trees, and HOB-CNN shows generalization across different trees and robustness under different occluded conditions.
summary: Orchard automation has attracted the attention of researchers recently due to the shortage of global labor force. To automate tasks in orchards such as pruning, thinning, and harvesting, a detailed understanding of the tree structure is required. However, occlusions from foliage and fruits can make it challenging to predict the position of occluded trunks and branches. This work proposes a regression-based deep learning model, Hallucination of Occluded Branch Convolutional Neural Network (HOB-CNN), for tree branch position prediction in varying occluded conditions. We formulate tree branch position prediction as a regression problem towards the horizontal locations of the branch along the vertical direction or vice versa. We present comparative experiments on Y-shaped trees with two state-of-the-art baselines, representing common approaches to the problem. Experiments show that HOB-CNN outperform the baselines at predicting branch position and shows robustness against varying levels of occlusion. We further validated HOB-CNN against two different types of 2D trees, and HOB-CNN shows generalization across different trees and robustness under different occluded conditions.
abstract_zh: 由于全球劳动力短缺，果园自动化近年来引起了研究人员的关注。为了自动化果园中的修剪、间伐和收获等任务，需要对树木结构有详细的了解。然而，叶子和果实的遮挡可能会使预测被遮挡的树干和树枝的位置变得困难。这项工作提出了一种基于回归的深度学习模型，即遮挡分支卷积神经网络幻觉（HOB-CNN），用于不同遮挡条件下的树枝位置预测。我们将树枝位置预测制定为沿着垂直方向朝向分支水平位置的回归问题，反之亦然。我们对具有两个最先进基线的 Y 形树进行了比较实验，代表了解决该问题的常见方法。实验表明，HOB-CNN 在预测分支位置方面优于基线，并且对不同程度的遮挡表现出鲁棒性。我们进一步针对两种不同类型的 2D 树验证了 HOB-CNN，HOB-CNN 显示了跨不同树的泛化能力以及不同遮挡条件下的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于回归的深度学习模型HOB-CNN，用于在2D果树图像中预测被遮挡的枝干位置。
AI.motivation: 果园自动化任务（如修剪、采摘）需要准确理解树体结构，但叶片和果实的遮挡导致枝干位置预测困难，亟需鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 将枝干位置预测建模为水平方向与垂直方向的回归问题，设计HOB-CNN网络通过回归方法预测被遮挡枝干位置，并在Y型树和两种不同类型2D树上进行验证。
AI.result: HOB-CNN在枝干位置预测精度上优于两种先进基线方法，且在不同遮挡程度下表现稳定，具有跨树种的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: HOB-CNN有效解决了遮挡问题，为果园自动化提供了鲁棒的树体结构分析方法，可支持精准农业任务。

authors_text: Muhammad Asad,Reuben Dorent,Tom Vercauteren
title: FastGeodis: Fast Generalised Geodesic Distance Transform
title_zh: FastGeodis：快速广义测地距离变换
comment: Accepted at Journal of Open Source Software (JOSS)
comments: Accepted at Journal of Open Source Software (JOSS)
abstract: The FastGeodis package provides an efficient implementation for computing Geodesic and Euclidean distance transforms (or a mixture of both), targeting efficient utilisation of CPU and GPU hardware. In particular, it implements the paralellisable raster scan method from Criminisi et al. (2009), where elements in a row (2D) or plane (3D) can be computed with parallel threads. This package is able to handle 2D as well as 3D data, where it achieves up to a 20x speedup on a CPU and up to a 74x speedup on a GPU as compared to an existing open-source library (Wang, 2020) that uses a non-parallelisable single-thread CPU implementation. The performance speedups reported here were evaluated using 3D volume data on an Nvidia GeForce Titan X (12 GB) with a 6-Core Intel Xeon E5-1650 CPU. Further in-depth comparison of performance improvements are discussed in the FastGeodis documentation: https://fastgeodis.readthedocs.io
summary: The FastGeodis package provides an efficient implementation for computing Geodesic and Euclidean distance transforms (or a mixture of both), targeting efficient utilisation of CPU and GPU hardware. In particular, it implements the paralellisable raster scan method from Criminisi et al. (2009), where elements in a row (2D) or plane (3D) can be computed with parallel threads. This package is able to handle 2D as well as 3D data, where it achieves up to a 20x speedup on a CPU and up to a 74x speedup on a GPU as compared to an existing open-source library (Wang, 2020) that uses a non-parallelisable single-thread CPU implementation. The performance speedups reported here were evaluated using 3D volume data on an Nvidia GeForce Titan X (12 GB) with a 6-Core Intel Xeon E5-1650 CPU. Further in-depth comparison of performance improvements are discussed in the FastGeodis documentation: https://fastgeodis.readthedocs.io
abstract_zh: FastGeodis 包提供了计算测地线和欧几里得距离变换（或两者的混合）的有效实现，目标是有效利用 CPU 和 GPU 硬件。特别是，它实现了 Criminisi 等人的并行光栅扫描方法。 (2009)，其中行 (2D) 或平面 (3D) 中的元素可以使用并行线程计算。该软件包能够处理 2D 和 3D 数据，与使用不可并行单线程 CPU 实现的现有开源库（Wang，2020）相比，它在 CPU 上实现了高达 20 倍的加速，在 GPU 上实现了高达 74 倍的加速。此处报告的性能加速是使用 Nvidia GeForce Titan X (12 GB) 和 6 核 Intel Xeon E5-1650 CPU 上的 3D 体积数据进行评估的。 FastGeodis 文档中讨论了性能改进的进一步深入比较：https://fastgeodis.readthedocs.io

AI.tldr: 本文提出FastGeodis包，实现高效的广义测地距离变换算法，支持2D/3D数据在CPU和GPU上的快速计算。
AI.motivation: 现有开源库采用单线程非并行化方法计算距离变换，处理大规模3D数据时效率不足。
AI.method: 基于Criminisi等人提出的可并行化栅格扫描算法，通过多线程并行计算行（2D）或平面（3D）元素。
AI.result: 在Nvidia Titan X GPU和Intel Xeon CPU上分别实现74倍和20倍速度提升，优于Wang（2020）的单线程实现。
AI.conclusion: FastGeodis通过硬件加速显著提升了距离变换计算效率，适用于大规模医学影像等3D数据分析场景。

search_text: FastGeodis: Fast Generalised Geodesic Distance Transform FastGeodis：快速广义测地距离变换 The FastGeodis package provides an efficient implementation for computing Geodesic and Euclidean distance transforms (or a mixture of both), targeting efficient utilisation of CPU and GPU hardware. In particular, it implements the paralellisable raster scan method from Criminisi et al. (2009), where elements in a row (2D) or plane (3D) can be computed with parallel threads. This package is able to handle 2D as well as 3D data, where it achieves up to a 20x speedup on a CPU and up to a 74x speedup on a GPU as compared to an existing open-source library (Wang, 2020) that uses a non-parallelisable single-thread CPU implementation. The performance speedups reported here were evaluated using 3D volume data on an Nvidia GeForce Titan X (12 GB) with a 6-Core Intel Xeon E5-1650 CPU. Further in-depth comparison of performance improvements are discussed in the FastGeodis documentation: https://fastgeodis.readthedocs.io FastGeodis 包提供了计算测地线和欧几里得距离变换（或两者的混合）的有效实现，目标是有效利用 CPU 和 GPU 硬件。特别是，它实现了 Criminisi 等人的并行光栅扫描方法。 (2009)，其中行 (2D) 或平面 (3D) 中的元素可以使用并行线程计算。该软件包能够处理 2D 和 3D 数据，与使用不可并行单线程 CPU 实现的现有开源库（Wang，2020）相比，它在 CPU 上实现了高达 20 倍的加速，在 GPU 上实现了高达 74 倍的加速。此处报告的性能加速是使用 Nvidia GeForce Titan X (12 GB) 和 6 核 Intel Xeon E5-1650 CPU 上的 3D 体积数据进行评估的。 FastGeodis 文档中讨论了性能改进的进一步深入比较：https://fastgeodis.readthedocs.io 本文提出FastGeodis包，实现高效的广义测地距离变换算法，支持2D/3D数据在CPU和GPU上的快速计算。 Muhammad Asad,Reuben Dorent,Tom Vercauteren cs.CV,eess.IV
